title: PatchNet-BiLSTM: Lightweight Sequential Model for Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
authors: Tanzim Redwan, Dhanya Sudarsan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is one of the major causes of preventable blindness and needs to be screened using accurate yet efficient methods. Recent deep learning architectures are either very accurate at the cost of computational complexity or lack interpretability. This study presents PatchNet-BiLSTM, a hybrid architecture that divides fundus images into patches, extracts local lesion characteristics using CNNs, and models the spatial inter dependencies between them using BiLSTM units. Tested on the APTOS 2019 Blindness Detection dataset, the model attained 96.4% accuracy, 96.42% precision, 96.4% recall, and 96.4% F1-score, surpassing a number of CNN and hybrid baselines with efficiency. The findings point to PatchNet-BiLSTM as a promising solution for clinical decision support and lowresource DR screening.

title: Self-Aware Risk Perception and Adaptive Safety Control for Autonomous Mining Trucks
authors: Qi Xia, Peng Chen, Xiao Chen, Guoyan Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Ensuring the driving safety of autonomous mining trucks (AMT) in unstructured mining environment remains challenging due to irregular road boundaries, unmarked lanes, and irregularly shaped obstacles. This study proposes an autonomous risk perception and safety control method designed for such environment. A spatial risk field and a collision risk field are constructed to autonomously quantify multi-source risk, and an adaptive control strategy is designed based on risk levels, forming an integrated framework from self-aware risk assessment to adaptive safety control. The proposed method is validated using a mining-specific simulator. In normal obstacle encounter scenarios, the AMT maintains an effective safe distance of over 15 m from obstacles. In abnormal deviation scenarios, it can promptly identify risk and stop effectively. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can accurately assess risk and adaptively control the AMT speed, effectively ensuring the driving safety of AMT in unstructured mining environment.

title: 3D-Chatbot: A Intelligent Conversational Agent Integrating RAG, NLP, And VR For Commercial Stores
authors: Telmo Sampaio, Pedro Filipe Oliveira, Paulo Matos
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive 3D chatbot system that combines advanced AI capabilities with practical commercial applications in immersive virtual reality environments. The system integrates natural language processing, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architectures, and real-time speech processing to create seamless customer inquiry experiences. The implementation combines four main components: an intelligent data acquisition pipeline that transforms over 1,000 unstructured web documents into AI-ready knowledge bases; an advanced RAG system utilizing OpenAI’s text-embedding-3-small model and Pinecone vector database for semantic search; multimodal speech processing with real-time speech-to-text conversion and lip-sync animation; and a Unity-based VR environment with professional avatar interactions. The results demonstrate exceptional performance with a 94.6% success rate across 65 test interactions, achieving response times under 100 milliseconds for knowledge retrieval and completing full voice response cycles in less than 3 seconds. The system maintains conversation context through a six-step interaction flow while accessing external knowledge sources, with 96.9% contextually appropriate responses and 92.3% conversation continuity in multi-turn dialogues. Performance optimizations ensure responsive operation under variable load conditions, demonstrating the viability of intelligent virtual assistants in three-dimensional commercial spaces.

title: Assistant for medical appointments and medication recommendations using Chatbot with NLP techniques in the healthcare sector in Lima
authors: Ronnie Gonzales, Angelo Contreras, Roberto Montero
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Health is a vital aspect of a country, and the demand for medical care increases in line with population growth. However, current care channels are overwhelmed due to the high demand for health services. Waiting times for medical care or routine medications for any treatment are excessive. To address this situation, we propose the development of a chatbot that uses natural language processing techniques to streamline the process as a virtual assistant in the healthcare sector. This new intelligent care channel is designed to facilitate faster and more efficient medical appointment booking. This allows the assistant to offer personalized recommendations for the medical area needed based on the symptoms reported by the patient, as well as information on available services and medications. In practice, based on a significant sample of 25 patients, the application achieved an overall satisfaction rate of 80% with the care provided, 84% with ease of use, and improved service time by up to 69% compared to traditional channels, findings that demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposal.

title: Development of an IoT-Enabled CNC Paper Cuttng System using Integrated QFD-Design Thinking Approach
authors: Nicholas Yohanes Pambudi, Ezra Peranginangin, Nina Tania Lestari
abstract: The packaging industry is undergoing significant digital transformation, driven by sustainability demands and the need for rapid prototyping capabilities. This research presents the development of an IoT-enabled Computer Numerical Control (CNC) paper cutting system specifically designed for packaging design services, integrating Quality Function Deployment (QFD) with Design Thinking methodology. The system addresses critical challenges in packaging prototype development, particularly the transition from manual to automated cutting processes for eco-friendly paper-based materials. Through qualitative user research and systematic design evaluation, we developed a CNC system featuring real-time monitoring capabilities, cloud-based design integration, and predictive maintenance through IoT sensors. The prototype demonstrates significant improvements in cutting precision (±0.1mm tolerance), production time reduction (70% faster than manual methods), and enables remote operation monitoring. This research contributes to Industry 4.0 implementation in packaging services, offering a comprehensive framework for integrating digital technologies in traditional manufacturing processes while maintaining focus on user-centered design principles.

title: Virtual Arrays for ISAC MIMO OTFS Systems with Rectangular Pulses
authors: Kailong Wang, Athina Petropulu
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is a key paradigm for improving resource efficiency in wireless networks by merging sensing and communication functionalities. Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation exhibits robustness in the high-Doppler environments, which are common to future high-mobility applications. In this paper, we propose a novel ISAC-OTFS system based on a monostatic multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) radar configuration, which enables a flexible trade-off between communication and sensing. This tradeoff is achieved by reserving a small subset of time–frequency (TF) domain resources as private for specific transmit antennas. Signals received on these private bins are used to form a virtual array (VA), thereby enhancing angle resolution. However, the introduction of private bins reduces the number of Doppler–delay (DD) domain bins available for data communication, establishing the fundamental trade-off between sensing performance and communication rate. Our design incorporates a non-ideal rectangular pulse shaping function at both the transmitter and receiver, and reformulates our previous work—originally based on ideal, bi-orthogonal pulses—to account for the interference introduced in this practical scenario. Simulation results validate that the proposed framework achieves high sensing performance comparable to systems using ideal pulses, with only a small impact on communication performance.

title: Human Trajectory Recovery Network Based on Trajectory-User Linking
authors: Liang Song, Peiying Zeng, Yongxuan Lai, Fuhua Fan, Wei Zhou, Fei Liu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Human trajectory recovery aims to mine historical trajectories and leverage their correlation with current trajectories to predict missing points at given times. Existing trajectory recovery models tend to focus on characteristics of trajectories, incorporating spatiotemporal dependencies before and after missing points, while neglecting exploration of user-specific preferences. Linking trajectories to their corresponding users and leveraging specific user behavior patterns for precise prediction not only enhances tasks like trajectory recovery and prediction, but also facilitates the extraction of user contextual features, enabling a deeper understanding of semantic information within user trajectories. Based on this, this paper proposes a Human Mobility Trajectory Recovery Network based on Trajectory-User Linking(GVAE-TUL). The trajectory encoding extracted by this network, enriched with user features, can be integrated into multiple trajectory recovery model frameworks to enhance the semantic representation of user characteristics. Experiments conducted on three real-world datasets validate the effectiveness of GVAE-TUL and demonstrate its ability to improve the performance of trajectory recovery models.

title: The Rise of Digital Technology in Cognitive Impairment with Older People: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Xule Zhu, Shihua Cao, Yuyang Jia, Shiying Zhou, Xinyu Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Object: To analyze the current status and frontier trends of digital technology application in the field of older people with cognitive impairment. Methods: Relevant articles focusing on research related to the application of digital technology in geriatric cognitive impairment were retrieved in the Web of Science database from its inception to June 25, 2025. The retrieved articles then subjected to visual analysis using CiteSpace 6.4.1. Results: A total of 556 articles were included. The annual number of publications showed an overall upward trend. The United States was a country with high literature production. Arlati was the scholar who has published the most articles, and Aristotle University of Thessaloniki and University of London were the high-yield institutions. High-frequency keywords included Alzheimer’s disease, virtual reality, dementia, older adults, mild cognitive impairment. Bursting keywords appearing in the past two years were quality of life and individuals. Conclusion: The research on the application of digital technology in the field of older people with cognitive impairment was generally on the rise, and the research hotspots included digital health, serious games and virtual reality. Systematic analysis of their mechanism and practical application results were expected to become important research trend in the future.

title: Matrix representation of basic quantum circuits
authors: Pedro Juan Roig, Salvador Alcaraz, Katja Gilly, Cristina Bernad, Blas Trigueros
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Quantum computing fundamentals are based on Hilbert spaces and linear algebra, which allows to describe quantum gates as matrix representations. In this paper, a matrix approach is taken in order to analyze basic quantum circuits, composed of basic quantum gates of 1, 2, and 3 qubits. The most common cases of all those gates are analyzed. One-qubit gates are expressed as 2x2 matrices, accounting for a rotation around a determined axis. Two-qubit gates are described as 4x4 matrices, standing for controlled gates. Three-quibit gates are defined as 8x8 matrices, extending the concept of controlled gates and rotations.

title: Spatiotemporal-Modal Cognitive Agents Based on Programmatic Reasoning
authors: Qing Yu, Tong Jing, Bin Zhang, Xuren Deng, Yunshan Lv, Heping Zhang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) remain largely inept at directly handling spatiotemporal-modal data, and thus struggle with spatiotemporal computation and iterative, tool-assisted tasks. This artical introduces a programmatic-reasoning–enhanced framework for spatiotemporal-modal cognitive agents that decouples spatiotemporal computation from textual reasoning, markedly improving LLM performance on complex spatiotemporal analytics. We design a high-discipline prompting template—PlanTemplate—to elicit programmatic reasoning (PoT, Program of Thoughts), optimize PoT-style program-augmented prompting, and adopt the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to standardize tool encapsulation and automated execution. Experiments show that, relative to standard Chain-of-Thought (CoT), our approach improves accuracy by 40% on GSM-Hard and 18.6% on Terra benchmarks; first-attempt completion on spatiotemporal tasks reaches 55.6 – 67%, and response latency decreases by 67.5%. The study provides both theoretical underpinnings and practical guidance for deploying LLMs in urban spatiotemporal intelligence.

title: Radiation-Induced Degradation of Electrical Parameters in SOI n-MOSFETs
authors: Dorsaf Aguir, Mohsen Machhout
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of gamma radiation on the electrical behaviour of SOI n-channel MOSFETs with a 1 µm channel length. Current–voltage (I–V) characteristics were measured before and after exposure to increasing Total Ionizing Dose (TID) levels up to 348 Krad. The results show a negative threshold voltage shift, a transconductance degradation in the linear regime, and an increase in drain current in saturation. In addition, small-signal parameters such as voltage/current gains, input/output impedances, and cut-off frequency exhibit significant variations with TID, especially under low gate biases. A compact DC model and a small-signal hybrid model are proposed to explain the observed behaviour, incorporating oxide charge trapping and interface state generation. These findings contribute to the development of radiation-hardened MOSFETs for applications in space and nuclear environments.

title: Watt Mechanism-Driven Fully Passive Robot Finger for Pinching and Scooping Tasks
authors: Xinru Yao, Tunan Zhangyu, Wenzeng Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: With the advancement of humanoid robotics, the challenge of robotic manipulation under environmental constraints has drawn increasing attention. Conventional industrial grippers often perform poorly when grasping flat objects. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel WYZY Hand that integrates a Watt mechanism with a double parallelogram mechanism. Designed with a three-phalanx structure mimicking the human finger, the hand supports three grasping modes: parallel pinching, scooping, and adaptive grasping, enabling composite manipulation strategies. Through passive downward contact with a desktop, the hand automatically switches from parallel pinching to scooping mode upon reaching a certain height via finger-table interaction, thereby effectively gripping flat objects. The adaptive mode accommodates desktops of various shapes, while compound grasping allows handling of irregular objects. This paper presents kinematic and dynamic analyses, establishes a detailed dynamic model for each linkage, and examines the force transmission mechanism. The range of grasping force is derived, and safety validation is conducted. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed hand achieves effective and stable performance in grasping diverse objects, offering a viable solution to manipulation tasks under environmental constraints.

title: A Wind Power Forecasting Paradigm Incorporating the Temporal-Frequency-Enhanced Training Strategy
authors: Yugui Tang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Multi-step wind power forecasting serves as a vital technique for maintaining a smart grid where wind energy is embedded. However, traditional direct forecasting paradigm suffers from a fundamental contradiction between label independence assumption and label autocorrelation in wind power. Therefore, this study proposes a novel wind power forecasting paradigm incorporating a Temporal-Frequency-enhanced training strategy. Specifically, a dual-component loss function incorporating temporal-domain accuracy and frequency-domain consistency is proposed for mitigating label autocorrelations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed forecasting paradigm significantly outperforms benchmark models in terms of forecasting accuracy. Furthermore, compared with traditional direct forecasting paradigm, the Temporal-Frequency-enhanced training strategy yields accuracy improvements of up to 7.73% and 6.06% in deep learning framework and transfer learning framework, respectively. The study provides a simple yet effective insight into improving multi-step wind power forecasting performance.

title: Design and Implementation of a Conditioning Circuit for an IDT Sensor
authors: Siwar Bellahirich, Amel Essid, Fatma Khayat, Haykel Bouaziz, Mohamed Hadj Said, Ayoub Haj Said
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: Data collection, storage, analysis and processing is a very promising and essential field for scientific progress, therefore, the continuous development of data acquisition processes is essential. In this context, this work proposes the implementation of a data acquisition and transfer platform based on an IDT interdigital sensor aiming to fit into the world of the Internet of Things (IoT) applied to the measurement and interface of physical quantities. Traditional sensor conditioning methods do not allow to achieve efficient results with the adopted IDT structure known for its very high sensitivity, real time monitoring and compact design capable of being miniaturized, hence, the need to design a new robust and reliable method capable of transmitting very low measured interdigital capacitances. Thus, a conditioning circuit based on an ESP32 card is interposed in addition to an integrated program in order to estimate the value of the attribute via its charge and discharge curve and transfer the measured values via Wi-Fi (Firebase) and Bluetooth. On the other hand, a dynamic web graphical interface is created providing real-time visualization and storage of curves and digital measurements in the database while displaying them in parallel on an LCD display. Thanks to this research work, collecting and transferring low interdigital capacitances issue is solved and it is now possible to benefit from them in required applications.

title: JDPNet: A Network Based on Joint Degradation Processing for Underwater Image Enhancement
authors: Tao Ye, Hongbin Ren, Chongbing Zhang, Haoran Chen, Xiaosong Li
abstract: Given the complexity of underwater environments and the variability of water as a medium, underwater images are inevitably subject to various types of degradation. The degradations present nonlinear coupling rather than simple superposition, which renders the effective processing of such coupled degradations particularly challenging. Most existing methods focus on designing specific branches, modules, or strategies for specific degradations, with little attention paid to the potential information embedded in their coupling. Consequently, they struggle to effectively capture and process the nonlinear interactions of multiple degradations from a bottom-up perspective. To address this issue, we propose JDPNet, a joint degradation processing network, that mines and unifies the potential information inherent in coupled degradations within a unified framework. Specifically, we introduce a joint feature-mining module, along with a probabilistic bootstrap distribution strategy, to facilitate effective mining and unified adjustment of coupled degradation features. Furthermore, to balance color, clarity, and contrast, we design a novel AquaBalanceLoss to guide the network in learning from multiple coupled degradation losses. Experiments on six publicly available underwater datasets, as well as two new datasets constructed in this study, show that JDPNet exhibits state-of-the-art performance while offering a better tradeoff between performance, parameter size, and computational cost.

title: LSTM-based Spatial-Temporal Prediction of Child Stunting Using Pearson Correlation Feature Selection
authors: Rosalina Yani Widiastuti, Danny Manongga, Irwan Sembiring, Helti Lygia Mampouw
abstract: Child stunting is a major public health challenge that requires accurate prediction models integrating spatial and temporal dimensions. This study develops a predictive framework that combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with Pearson Correlation Coefficient (PCC) feature selection and Attention Gate (ATG) mechanisms. The goal is to improve prediction accuracy and model interpretability.We analyzed 398 annual records from 41 health centers in Banyumas Regency, Indonesia, covering the years 2019-2024. The data were transformed into longitudinal village-level panel data. The proposed LSTM+ATG model, effectively captures medium-term temporal patterns and spatial heterogeneity across different geographical contexts.Experimental results show that LSTM+ATG outperforms baseline models such as linear regression, conventional LSTM, Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), and Random Forest, with RMSE of X, MAE of Y, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of Z. The model maintains stable performance while providing interpretable attention weights for feature importance.This approach demonstrates strong potential as a decision support system for precise, adaptive, and evidence-based stunting intervention planning at the local government level.

title: ISOCAL-DDMR - Isostatic Calibration Platform for Differential Drive Mobile Robots
authors: Darci Luiz Tomasi, Eduardo Todt
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Odometric accuracy remains a key limitation in Differential Drive Mobile Robots (DDMRs), as small structural inaccuracies or wheel slippage accumulate and cause localization errors. Traditional calibration methods, such as the UMBmark procedure, are reliable but demand large physical spaces and controlled conditions rarely available in research or educational settings. To overcome these constraints, this paper presents ISOCAL-DDMR - a novel controlled experimental platform integrating isostatic structural principles for dynamic wheelbase measurement. Unlike conventional static methods, ISOCAL-DDMR continuously estimates the wheel separation in real time, ensuring greater physical consistency of odometric parameters. Built upon open-source hardware and software, it employs an ESP32 microcontroller interfaced with LS7366R counters and HX711 strain-gauge converters for deterministic pulse acquisition and structural monitoring. Experiments with the DN2 robotic base show that the proposed method reduces systematic errors and achieves statistical equivalence with real-environment displacements, as verified through Welch's t-tests (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p > 0.05$</tex>). To the best of the authors' knowledge, ISOCAL-DDMR provides a novel and accessible solution for reproducible odometry calibration, bridging the gap between theory and experimental practice.

title: Edge-Enabled IoT-Based Dynamic Traffic Management System
authors: Faten Ben Abdallah, Sarra Ben Halima, Farah Mahmoud
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This paper presents an edge-enabled IoT framework for dynamic urban traffic management that computes and enforces context-aware speed recommendations in real time. Vehicles supply on-board diagnostics (OBD-II) data to an embedded edge device (Raspberry Pi), which performs local pre-processing to extract speed and density indicators and publishes compact messages via MQTT secured with TLS to a cloud service. The cloud executes a lightweight control model that estimates an optimal speed v<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">opt</inf> as a function of traffic density, congestion index, road priority, and weather, and disseminates the result to roadside Variable Speed Limit Signs (VSLS). The pipeline operates with periodic updates and event-triggered refresh. Experiments in simulated urban scenarios, complemented by short field tests, show improved throughput (+30%), reduced speed variability (−57%), higher average travel speed (+16%), and lower fuel consumption (−17%), with an estimated 15–20% reduction in NOx emissions. End-to-end decision latency averages 320ms, with under 100ms at the edge. These results indicate that combining in-vehicle edge processing with secure cloud coordination yields timely, scalable traffic harmonization while limiting bandwidth and energy costs on constrained devices.

title: Beamforming with Phase Center Control for Precision GNSS
authors: Jan Steckel, Noori Bni-Lam
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: Accurate control of the phase center in antenna arrays is critical for high-precision applications like GNSS, where small displacements can cause significant localization errors. Conventional beamforming often overlooks phase center variation, leading to spatial shifts. We introduce Phase-Center-Constrained Beamforming (PCCB), a novel algorithm that minimizes phase center offset (PCO) while maintaining directional gain. Formulated as a constrained optimization problem with regularization for energy compactness and beampattern fidelity, PCCB enables gain control and interference nulling with significantly reduced PCO displacement. Simulations show a fivefold reduction in PCO shift compared to traditional methods. Stability analysis across random initializations confirms the robustness and consistency of PCCB, making it a practical solution for minimizing phase center variability.

title: Correlation Between P300 Latency and Digit Span Performance in Primary School Children: A Cross-Sectional ERP Study
authors: Suzy Yusna Dewi, Nasywa Khairunnisa Kusuma, Sri Wahyuningsih, Yazid Habiburahman, Herdan Listianto, Yasmin Azizah Idhsa, Galuh Dwi Rahmawati, Abdul Wahab Abdul Rahman
abstract: Learning difficulties are often experienced by elementary school children and frequently not detected early. This happens due to the lack of early screening to assess the intelligence aspects of children that influence attention and memory. In Jakarta, 13,7% of elementary school students aged around 6-12 years old experiencing learning difficulties and 8.04% of students experiencing learning difficulties with deficits in working memory. Currently, intelligence test and cognitive function are often assessed when children have serious learning problems that hinder their academic performance. The P300 wave, generated by a technology called Event-Related Potentials (ERPS), is used for screening when there are attention and memory issues in children with learning difficulties. The measurement of forward and backward on the digit span test (DS) is also one of the most widely used neuropsychological tests to assess attention and short-term memory in children. This study examined the relationship between P300 latency and the degree of attention and memory using digit span test in elementary school children. This study was used an analytical correlational design with cross-sectional approach. The sample used in this study was 61 elementary school students aged around 6-10 years who met the inclusion criteria, selected using a proportional random sampling technique. The result show a significant correlation between P300 latency at the Cz electrode and the digit span test (r = 0.312, p = 0.015). In contrast, there is no significant correlation was found between P300 latency at the Fz electrode and rhe digit span test (r = 0.038, p = 0.559). This study reveals a significant positive correlation between the P300 latency at the Cz electrode and the digit span test in subjects, which may be influenced by brain maturation and cognitive function in child development..

title: Real-Time Web-Based Emotion Recognition Using Hybrid CNN-LSTM for Audience Analytics
authors: Dedy Prasetya Kristiadi, Lukman Nulhakim, Muhaimin Hasanudin
abstract: Evaluating audience emotional responses to video content is critical for marketing and user experience optimization, yet current methods rely on subjective surveys prone to bias. We present a real-time, web-based emotion recognition system that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spatial feature extraction with Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) networks for temporal modeling of facial expressions. Implemented using TensorFlow.js for client-side processing, our system ensures user privacy while enabling real-time analysis. We evaluated the system on 50 participants across diverse demographic backgrounds, achieving 87.3% accuracy in classifying seven basic emotions (happy, sad, angry, surprised, fearful, disgusted, neutral) and 76.8% accuracy in emotion-based content recommendations. The multimodal approach, incorporating head motion cues, improved recognition accuracy by 12.4% compared to facial features alone. Our web-based implementation demonstrates the feasibility of deploying complex deep learning models for real-time affect recognition without server-side processing, with implications for digital marketing, UX design, and audience analytics.

title: ANGELS: An Intelligent Gestational Follow-up System
authors: Élisson Da Silva Rocha, Cleber Matos De Morais, Igor Vitor Teixeira, Kayo Henrique De Carvalho Monteiro, Sebastião Rogério Da Silva Neto, Wembley Carvalho, Helio Rubens Soares, Rubem Saldanha, Waldemar Brandão Neto, Patricia Takako Endo
abstract: The reduction of fetal, neonatal, and infant mortality rates is crucial in maternal and neonatal care. The UN’s 2030 Agenda aims for a better world by 2030, highlighting goal 3: healthy lives for all ages, with a focus on reducing global maternal and child mortality. In this context, prenatal care plays a vital role in identifying pregnant women at risk and enabling proactive interventions to minimize adverse outcomes, whether mortality or morbidity. This work presents the ANGELS (An iNtelligent GEstational foLlow-up System). This work presents ANGELS, an intelligent gestational follow-up platform designed to integrate and operationalize multiple machine learning models within a unified decision support system for maternal and neonatal care. The platform provides integrated services through a modular, API-based architecture, enabling the incorporation of different predictive models throughout pregnancy, childbirth, and postpartum periods. The system is validated in this study through an exemplar case focused on congenital syphilis risk prediction using real-world Brazilian data, demonstrating howpreviously validated models can be integrated, extended, and deployed within the ANGELS platform. In the exemplar case of congenital syphilis risk prediction, the complementary version of ANGELS achieved substantial improvements, with specificity and precision increasing from approximately 60% in the basic version to values close to 95%, reflecting known trade-offs across evaluation metrics. The approach proved promising for practical incorporation into healthcare systems, aiming to provide more accurate and personalized monitoring tailored to individual needs. It is hoped that the ANGELS system may contribute to reduce preventable stillbirths, improve the quality of maternal and child care, and help achieve the UN’s 2030 Agenda goals. Additionally, the integration of ANGELS services into healthcare systems and/or programs will strengthen efforts to promote the well-being of pregnant women and children.

title: STDG-FCM: Spatial and Texture Dual-Guided Fuzzy C-Means for Single-Point Supervised Infrared Small Target Mask Generation_supp1-3673061.docx
authors: Qingyu Hou
abstract: Infrared small target (IRST) detection is significantly hindered by the high cost and ambiguity of pixel-level annotations, especially for dim targets in low signal-toclutter ratio (SCR) scenes. To alleviate the annotation burden, we propose a spatial and texture dual-guided fuzzy C-means (STDG-FCM) framework for generating pseudo-masks under single-point supervision. STDG-FCM enhances region coherence through spatially weighted clustering and refines boundaries by employing a novel texture strategy based on the gray-level cooccurrence matrix (GLCM). Experiments conducted on three public datasets validate the superiority of STDG-FCM in generating high-quality pseudo-masks compared to existing methods, with an intersection over union (IoU) of 0.6578 on the SIRST dataset. Furthermore, we employ the generated masks in IRST detectors, achieve performance comparable to fully supervised training. This demonstrates that STDG-FCM is an efficient and practical mask annotation strategy.
