title: Calculating Energies of 1D Quantum Harmonic Oscillator with Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Huber Nieto-Chaupis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: We employ the simplest representation of perceptron and orthogonal polynomials, to derive the quantized energies of an one-dimension harmonic oscillator by using mathematical methodologies that are interpreted in terms of quantum language. In contrast to standard mechanisms based in the usage of annihilation and creation operators, the oscillator turns out to be quantized. Indeed, a generalization is presented, fact that might be interpreted as a path to quantize 2D and 3D oscillators. From the Schrödinger equation, the respective solution namely the wave function for present case is also estimated in a closed-form. In the present case, the well-known integer-order Bessel functions are employed. The resultant energy spectra are in agree with quantum mechanics calculations.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of Multi-dimensional Student portraits in the New Ecosystem of Digital Smart Teaching
authors: Wei Liu, Li Xu, Xiaohan Long, Xingzhi Chang, Juhua Yu
abstract: With the deepening of digital transformation in education, the construction and application of student portraits are becoming increasingly important. In order to comprehensively and objectively evaluate the comprehensive quality of students in the new teaching ecosystem, this study proposes a multi-dimensional method for constructing student portraits. Based on the self-built classroom data collection system, programming behavior data such as students' code files, debugging time, and code logs are collected in real time. By integrating five dimensions including basic attributes, learning behaviors, cognitive abilities, emotional attitudes, and development potential, a multi-dimensional student portrait model is constructed. In practical teaching applications, it has been found that the construction and application of multidimensional student portraits have significantly improved students' learning outcomes, while also helping teachers improve their control over the quality of course teaching.

title: Autonomous Agent (AutoAgent) for Self Driving through Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jayabrata Chowdhury, Vishruth Veerendranath, Suresh Sundaram, Narasimman Sundararajan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Recent advances in maneuver planning for autonomous vehicles leverage expert driving behavior but struggle to balance safety and comfort. We introduce AutoAgent, a Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based framework that aligns with human driving behavior while optimizing for safety and comfort. AutoAgent combines a predictive model and RL: the predictive model, trained on historical data, forecasts surrounding vehicles' positions and embeds them in context-aware grid maps for the RL agent. Evaluations on simulated RL environments derived from real-world datasets show that AutoAgent consistently improves driving experience, achieving safe, comfortable, and humanaligned maneuvers. Compared to a state-of-the-art imitation learning model in unseen scenarios, AutoAgent demonstrates superior performance in handling complex situations. An ablation study highlights the predictive model's critical role.

title: OWL: A Smart Monitoring System for the Elderly Using Skeleton Detection and WhatsApp Notifications
authors: Erwani Merry Sartika, Muhamad Dani Ardiansyah, Annisa Maizano Fahlevi, Leonnora Odelia, Jesyca Augusta, Fanuelsedek Silalahi, Elizabeth Wianto
abstract: The increasing number of elderly people in Indonesia presents significant challenges in health and safety, particularly the high risk of falls, which are among the leading causes of injury-related deaths. This study proposes OWL (Smart Monitoring System for Elderly), a monitoring solution combining skeleton detection using MediaPipe with automatic WhatsApp notifications via the Twilio API. The system classifies elderly activities into four categories (Active, Still, Idle, and Alert) in real time using only a standard webcam, without additional sensors. From ten trials, the system achieved 100% accuracy in detecting the four statuses based on time thresholds of ≤15 s, 15–45 s, and >45 s, supported by color indicators and automated alerts. An alert mechanism is integrated through local audio alarms and emergency messages sent to caregivers. Furthermore, an interactive owl character provides a more human-centered and comforting experience. Experimental results confirm reliable activity detection and timely alerts, showing OWL’s potential as an adaptive and user-friendly monitoring solution.

title: Construction and Application of Interdisciplinary Talent Evaluation System Based on CIPP
authors: Yulong Geng, Yuxuan Li, Xiaodong Yu
abstract: This study aims to address the critical challenge of cultivating interdisciplinary talent in China's AI era. By employing the Engineering Ethics course at Shanghai Sanda University as a case study, a comprehensive evaluation system based on the CIPP model was constructed. This system was subsequently applied to survey data from recent graduates of several application-oriented universities in Shanghai, to empirically examine the effectiveness of generative AI as an innovative educational tool in enhancing interdisciplinary innovative thinking and capabilities. The findings conclusively demonstrate the system's reliability in diagnosing pedagogical deficiencies and guiding curriculum optimization, thereby offering a significant theoretical and practical tool for advancing high-quality interdisciplinary talent development.

title: Robust UAV Localization in GNSS-Denied Environments via Efficient Visual Scene Matching–Inertial Fusion
authors: Zhipeng Zhang, Buhong Zhang, Meibo Lv, Sunhao Liu, Jingyuan Xue, Hairui Wei
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: In environments where Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) are degraded or unavailable, unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) visual-inertial localization faces challenges including cross-domain scale inconsistency, accumulated inertial drift, and limited onboard computing resources. To address these issues, we propose a lightweight visual–inertial localization framework for onboard platforms that combines low-rate absolute corrections from satellite–to–aerial visual scene matching with high-rate relative motion constraints from inertial measurement unit (IMU) pre-integration. The two are fused via a loosely coupled Error-State Kalman Filter (ESKF), achieving real-time, robust localization through low-rate absolute correction + high-rate dead reckoning. To reduce the cross-domain search space and mitigate scale mismatch, we introduce a barometer-assisted adaptive satellite-image loading and ground sampling distance (GSD) normalization pipeline that dynamically selects satellite tiles and aligns scale between satellite and onboard imagery. On the vision side, an accelerated XFeat model delivers high-quality feature extraction and matching; On the fusion side, the ESKF effectively suppresses inertial drift and improves resilience to visual outliers and anomalous conditions. The full system is deployed on a NVIDIA Orin Nano embedded platform and validated in real flight tests, demonstrating accurate and robust localization in complex real-world scenarios.

title: Terminal Sliding Mode-Based Trajectory Tracking of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles in Agricultural Spraying
authors: Wafa Tlili, Rabii Fessi, Soufiene Bouallègue
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: Improving agricultural productivity is a growing challenge to cover the world’s food needs. Precision agriculture is about improving agricultural resources using new and smart technologies. In this framework, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) as assistive robots are proven to be highly responsive tools in a wide range of applications, such as farms monitoring, disease treatment and crop spraying. In this paper, a Terminal Sliding Mode Control (TSMC) strategy is proposed for flight trajectories tracking that are planned for different agricultural-inspired spraying scenarios. The proposed agricultural spraying path planning and tracking further improves the process performance in terms of accuracy and autonomy. Simulation results and discussions demonstrate the effectiveness of the TSMC-based crop spraying approach in terms of fastness, precision and robustness of system responses. Findings contribute to improving the efficiency of UAV-assisted spraying systems in the precision agriculture.

title: GenAI Awareness for High School Students: Case Study of Nearby Schools Around Nusantara, Indonesia
authors: Maresha Caroline Wijanto, Oscar Karnalim, Yolla Margaretha, Santy Setiawan, Henky Lisan Suwarno, Krismanto Kusbiantoro
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) can help students learn new materials or even assist them in completing assessments. However, GenAI should be used responsibly and ethically. While such knowledge is common for higher education, it is less familiar to high school students. This study surveyed 357 high school students in Indonesia to understand such awareness through a specifically tailored survey. Unique to our study, the survey was conducted in areas far from the current capital of a developing country, specifically East Kalimantan, Indonesia. Using a validated 10-item questionnaire, we assessed students' familiarity with GenAI tools, understanding of ethical implications, and attitudes toward recommended uses. While students are relatively aware of GenAI, they remain neutral about many of the recommended uses of GenAI. Additional interventions might be needed to promote GenAI awareness.

title: Dynamic Modeling and Optimization of a UV Water Disinfection System Using PSO for Enhanced Bacterial Reduction
authors: Said Riahi, Naoufel Zitouni, Abdelkader Mami
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid framework combining dynamic system modeling and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to improve UV water disinfection. The reactor is modeled as a MIMO system, with flow rate (0.2–0.8 L/s) and UV intensity (5, 7, and 10 mW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) as inputs, and UV dose and bacterial reduction as outputs. A dynamic transfer function is identified from experimental data, and its parameters are optimized via PSO to minimize the difference between simulated and measured results. Validation at 10 mW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> shows high predictive accuracy, and further simulations confirm the model’s robustness in capturing the system’s nonlinear behavior. Combining dynamic modeling with PSO provides a robust predictive tool for UV systems. This approach offers a reliable and efficient method to optimize UV disinfection under varying operating conditions.

title: Scalable Higher-Order Topology Identification from Nodal Observations
authors: Ruben Wijnands, Andrea Cavallo, Borbála Hunyadi, Elvin Isufi, Geert Leus
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: This paper proposes a scalable method for identifying interactions in higher-order networks from observations of nodal processes. Finding such dependencies is important in many disciplines, including neuroscience, social influence modeling, and beyond. However, current approaches are either limited to extracting pairwise dependencies or struggle with scalability, as estimating higher-order dependencies becomes computationally prohibitive. To overcome these challenges, we introduce a tensorbased graph Volterra model that leverages low-rank decomposition techniques to estimate higher-order interactions efficiently. Our approach not only reduces computational and storage complexity but also acts as an implicit regularizer, improving network estimation in ill-posed settings. We validate our method through simulations and real data experiments, demonstrating competitive performance and enhanced scalability compared to existing techniques.

title: Symmetric Rank-One Quasi-Newton Methods for Deep Learning Using Cubic Regularization
authors: Aditya Ranganath, Mukesh Singhal, Roummel Marcia
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: Stochastic gradient descent and other first-order variants, such as Adam and AdaGrad, are commonly used in the field of deep learning because of their computational efficiency and low-storage memory requirements. However, these methods do not exploit curvature information. Consequently, iterates can converge to saddle points or poor local minima. In contrast, Quasi-Newton methods compute Hessian approximations, which can avoid these pitfalls with a comparable computational budget. In this paper, we propose using a limited-memory symmetric rank-one quasi-Newton approach which allows for indefinite Hessian approximations, enabling directions of negative curvature to be exploited. Furthermore, we use a modified adaptive regularization using cubics approach, which generates a sequence of cubic subproblems that have closed-form solutions with suitable regularization choices. We investigate the performance of our proposed method on autoencoders and feed-forward neural network models and compare our approach to state-of-the-art first-order adaptive stochastic methods.

title: Machine Learning-Based Detection of Rare Failure Events in Industrial Machinery
authors: Jihen Issaoui, Dorsaf Daldoul, Nadia Bahria, Imen Harbaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: In the industrial sector, machine rare failure event refers to infrequent breakdown of equipment that occurs unexpectedly and is difficult to predict due to limited historical data and its sporadic nature. This type of failure has a significant impact on the entire production process. As a result, manufacturers place great emphasis on monitoring manufacturing operations, including equipment health. However, detecting rare malfunctions which impact equipment health and process stability despite their infrequency remains a major challenge. In this context, monitoring the system’s health and enabling early fault detection are essential levers for minimizing downtime. Predictive maintenance has therefore emerged as a powerful strategy to prevent costly interruptions in modern manufacturing environments. In this paper, an approach for detecting machine rare failure event is presented, relying on the use of machine learning algorithms. Results demonstrate that the KNN classifier performed better than the others tested classifiers, as it displays the best ROC.

title: Comparative Analysis of 2D Deep Neural Network Architectures for Lung Nodule Detection
authors: Tanapon Kitmuti, Kanjanapan Sukvichai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Cancer remains a substantial global health burden and the leading cause of death worldwide. Early detection is critical to improving patient survival rates. Deep Learning (DL) has emerged as a transformative force in medical image analysis, particularly for tasks involving Computed Tomography (CT) scans, the optimal architectural choice for specific tasks remains a critical area of investigation. The best Deep Neural Network (DNN) architecture for real-world deployment in medical image processing is primarily determined by the trade-off between operational efficiency (inference speed) and detection accuracy. This research aims to analytical compare of four major 2D Deep Neural Network (DNN) object detection architectures, which are SSD-VGG16, EfficientDet, and YOLO, to provide empirically supported insights for model selection in high-stakes diagnostic tasks. The most impactful finding challenges standard evaluation protocols by analyzing model stability and learning dynamics. The training loss analysis demonstrates that mAP scores acquired at an arbitrary, fixed checkpoint are a poor reflection of an architecture's true potential. Such a framework is critical for assisting medical experts in accurately detecting and tracking nodule changes, resulting in better diagnosis and assessment of treatment response.

title: LLM-Assisted TOGAF Preliminary Phase for Student Digital Twins
authors: Handoko, Alfa Yohannis, Ariya Panna
abstract: This study presents the Prompt-to-Preliminary- Phase (P2P) framework, using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate TOGAF Preliminary Phase artifact generation for educational enterprise architecture. Applied to a Student Digital Twin (SDT) platform, the method achieved up to 80% faster drafting with strong clarity and relevance. Expert evaluation confirmed the efficiency of AI-assisted outputs while highlighting the need for human contextual oversight. The results demonstrate a hybrid governance model where LLMs enhance structural precision and traceability, and human expertise ensures ethical, pedagogical, and regulatory integrity in education-centered architecture development.

title: Comparative Analysis of JPDA and GM-PHD Methods for Dense Group Target Tracking
authors: Yan E. Pivnev, Vitaliy S. Antipenko
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: This paper presents the result of simulation modeling of motion, detection and tracking of a group in the presence of measurement noise, a comparative analysis of two common methods of group target tracking: the Joint Probabilistic Data Association (JPDA) filter and the Gaussian Mixture Probability Hypothesis Density (GM-PHD) filter. The GOSPA metric and the average calculation time per scan are used to evaluate the quality of the modeling. The results demonstrate that while JPDA correctly estimates the number of trajectories, its computational load increases exponentially with the number of objects in the group. In contrast, GM-PHD exhibits a linear increase in computation with data volume but fails to provide a consistent estimate of the number of closely spaced objects. Based on these findings, the study proposes a hybrid approach, leveraging GM-PHD for initial group detection and position estimation in cluttered environments, and JPDA for subsequent precise tracking of individual trajectories.

title: Employee Retention Prediction using Machine Learning Models
authors: Herminiño Lagunzad, Warren Panizales, Jigo Rafael Catamio, Angelica Gaviola, Joshua Sanchez, Edzel Balmaceda
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: This study develops and evaluates a leakage-safe attrition-risk model for faculty retention using a 5,778-record dataset with an approximately 12% leave rate. The pipeline performs schema checks, stratified $\mathbf{8 0} / \mathbf{2 0}$ holdout splitting, and training within a single encapsulated preprocessing flow (median imputation, one-hot encoding with unknown-category handling, zscore scaling), followed by class-weighted logistic regression and stratified 5-fold cross-validation for model and threshold selection. On the holdout test, the model achieved $\mathrm{TN}=1,013, \mathrm{FP}=4, \mathrm{FN}=0$, $\mathbf{T P} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 3 9}$, yielding Accuracy $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{9 9. 6 5 \%}$, Precision (Leave $) \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{9 7. 2 \%}$, Recall(Leave) $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$, $\mathbf{F 1} \approx \mathbf{9 8. 6 \%}$, and Specificity $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{9 9. 6 1 \%}$ indicating no missed leavers and minimal false alarms. Threshold analysis identifies a broad optimum at $\mathbf{t} \approx \mathbf{0. 5 0 - 0. 6 0}$ (Recall $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1. 0 0}$, Precision $\approx 0.97$; $\max F 1 \approx 0.986$); a high-certainty operating point at $\mathbf{t} \approx \mathbf{0. 7 0}$ achieves Precision $\boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1. 0 0}$ with a modest recall trade-off ($\approx 0.964$). Permutation importance highlights mentoring-program participation, academic rank, work-life balance, and educational qualifications as the most informative signals, with researchactivity indicators and department context providing additional lift. The results demonstrate that an interpretable, class-aware linear model, paired with principled preprocessing and threshold calibration, can deliver accurate, actionable early-warning signals for proactive retention workflows. We recommend deploying the calibrated pipeline with ongoing monitoring of base rate, calibration, and operating metrics, and using a two-tier triage policy (standard outreach at $t \approx 0.50-0.60$; high-priority at $t \geqslant$ 0.70) to align model outputs with institutional capacity and intervention goals.

title: Entrepreneurial Intentions Prediction of Dental Practitioners Using Spiking Neural Networks and Intelligent Survey Design
authors: Guoliang Tan, Junming Huang, Yijun Liu, Wujian Ye, Zexiao Liang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: With the increasing demand for dental healthcare and the rapid growth of private dental clinics in Guangdong (South China), understanding the entrepreneurial intentions of dental practitioners has become a topic of significant interest. Questionnaire-based analysis is a practical approach for exploring the underlying relationships between entrepreneurial intentions and various influencing factors. However, two major challenges remain: designing meaningful survey questions and analyzing the inherently non-linear, symbolic nature of questionnaire data.To address these challenges, we propose a novel computational framework based on Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) a brain-inspired model well-suited for sparse, binary-coded, and event-driven data such as survey responses. The proposed SNN-based Questionnaire Analysis Framework includes two key components: feature importance analysis for optimizing questionnaire design, and an SNN-based classifier for predicting dental practitioners’ entrepreneurial intentions. By leveraging the SNNs ability to model complex temporal and nonlinear interactions with high energy efficiency, our framework demonstrates superior performance in both interpretability and classification accuracy. Extensive experiments on real-world questionnaire data validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: LSTM-Based Forecasting Workflow for Container Supply and Demand in Maritime Logistics Operations
authors: Efe Yılmaz Taşyürek, Çağdaş Yılmaz, Mete Topgül, Cem Solmaz, Sevinç İlhan Omurca, Lütfü Çakıl
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Forecasting container demand and supply in maritime logistics plays a critical role in optimizing operational efficiency and resource allocation across global trade networks. This study proposes an LSTM-based time series forecasting framework designed to forecast container flow dynamics by analyzing real-world operational data characterized by high dimensionality and inherent noise. The dataset comprises hierarchical spatial structures encompassing multiple countries, regions, and depots distributed across different continents. To ensure representational consistency, forecasting models were trained at multiple temporal spectrums daily and weekly. Experimental findings demonstrated that daily and weekly models provided stable and accurate forecasts due to their denser data representation and improved temporal learning capacity. A complementary noise assessment using the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) metric revealed the stochastic nature of operational data, while MSLE and RMSLE evaluations confirmed the robustness of the proposed LSTM architecture across both demand and supply forecasting tasks, providing actionable insights to support capacity management and operational planning in maritime logistics.

title: Predictive Analytics for Academic Success: Orchestrating Digital Transformation using Big Data techniques for identifying Academic Risk
authors: Shamnad Mohamed Shaffi, Jezeena Nikarthil Sidhick, Sunish Vengathattil, Resmi Vijayan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Predictive analytics lends itself very well to enhancing student success in today’s data-driven academic world to identify those with academic risk early. This paper presents an analysis of how the application of big data techniques to education, including machine learning, data mining, and artificial intelligence, is becoming possible to predict students’ achievements and be proactive in this sense. Predictive models can be utilized by analyzing vast amounts of datasets that have demographic information, academic records, engagement metrics, behavioral patterns, and more to identify the latent risk factors, thus warning educators and support services on time. It then looks at different predictive methods and the accuracy and ethical suitability of different levels of prediction and uses examples of their practical applications across schools and universities. Moreover, the paper poses some implementation challenges, including data privacy, model bias, and institutional readiness. What we find is that, predictively, analytics can have a significant impact on student retention, student academic outcomes, and personalized learning strategies when used responsibly. Future directions include the integration of real-time learning analytics and adjusting adaptive interventions to enable equitable learning outcomes.

title: BA Hand: A Novel Gripper Based on Object Adaptability and Plane Environment Adaptability
authors: Wenjie Ma, Wenzeng Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of poor adaptability of traditional industrial grippers in unstructured scenarios, high cost of high-degree-of-freedom dexterous hands, and grasping limitations of traditional underactuated hands, this paper proposes the design of the “BA Hand” underactuated robotic hand. This design integrates the advantages of the Omega Hand and the KoreaTech-BLT Hand. It adopts the Co-terminal Dualshaft Link - Long Slot Structure to achieve plane environment adaptation and scooping function, and uses a belt to realize surface-contact adaptive grasping. The structure is simplified and can be 3D-printed to reduce costs. Through kinematic analysis, key parameters such as the tilt angle range of the hand are determined. Combined with force analysis, the boundary of state switching is quantified. Simulation verifies the rationality of the theory, and experiments demonstrate its multi-mode grasping ability, showing excellent grasping performance for objects of different shapes.

title: Design of Gaze Evaluation System in Driving Using Eye Tracking and Object Detection
authors: Jagat Thakulla, Hironori Hiraishi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a gaze evaluation and training system for beginner drivers, implemented using Unity and You Only Look Once (YOLO) v8x for real-time object detection. Objects that could not be recognized by YOLOv8x were annotated manually. The system automatically identifies regions to be observed as must-see areas and regions that should be avoided as must-not-see areas. The must-see areas are marked with green bounding boxes, whereas the must-not-see areas are marked with red bounding boxes. Two experiments were conducted to assess YOLO accuracy and score comparison (manual counting) respectively. The manual score comparison was then updated to automatic scoring system which helps to calculate the automatic score when the subject looks at the system under the surveillance of an eye tracking sensor. Experiments with beginner and experienced drivers revealed significant differences in gaze patterns, with experienced drivers exhibiting wider gaze movements. In contrast, beginner drivers normally focusing straight on the road, showing insufficient hazard checks while driving. Finally, comparative score evaluation with two different approaches were performed with beginner and experienced drivers. The findings of this paper support the development of training mechanisms to improve driving skill in beginners and to enhance safe driving.

title: Streaming Intelligence Real Time Fraud Detection in Retail Payments Using Machine Learning
authors: Gopalakrishnan Venkatasubbu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: The expansion of electronic payments in the retail sector has been accompanied by an increase in advanced payment frauds. Traditional rule based legacy static fraud detection methods, in most cases, proved inadequate to address dynamically changing and constantly evolving fraudster practices. This paper discusses an efficient real time fraud detection system through a combination of Machine Learning (ML) algorithms and real time data stream processing technology. The study is helpful in the sense that it has a constructed synthetic transaction data set which was specially designed and built to be utilized for the very specific purpose of simulating real retail payment scenarios. The data have 489 samples with columns of transaction amount, merchant category, time of day, and frequency of transactions. We test and use a subset of the ML algorithms, like Random Forest and Gradient Boosting, to identify fraud effectively with very low latency. The solution takes consumption transaction as an Apache Kafka stream simulated, real time feature creation, and scoring of transactions using trained ML models. It depicts how ensemble methods, of which Gradient Boosting is a fair example, are more precise in recall and accuracy and minimize false negatives and false positives to a significant degree. This research acknowledges the prospect of a streaming paradigm and sophisticated ML in developing adaptive and real time fraud detection systems of high value addition to the security framework of modern retail payment systems.

title: Accelerating the TOGAF Preliminary Phase: An Empirical Evaluation of LLM-Generated Artifacts in Banking
authors: Izaaz Azaam Syahalam, Alfa Ryano Yohannis, Teddy Mantoro

title: Automated Tuning for Diffusion Inverse Problem Solvers without Generative Prior Retraining
authors: Yaşar Utku Alçalar, Junno Yun, Mehmet Akçakaya
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: Diffusion/score-based models have recently emerged as powerful generative priors for solving inverse problems, including accelerated MRI reconstruction. While their flexibility allows decoupling the measurement model from the learned prior, their performance heavily depends on carefully tuned data fidelity weights, especially under fast sampling schedules with few denoising steps. Existing approaches often rely on heuristics or fixed weights, which fail to generalize across varying measurement conditions and irregular timestep schedules. In this work, we propose Zero-shot Adaptive Diffusion Sampling (ZADS), a testtime optimization method that adaptively tunes fidelity weights across arbitrary noise schedules without requiring retraining of the diffusion prior. ZADS treats the denoising process as a fixed unrolled sampler and optimizes fidelity weights in a selfsupervised manner using only undersampled measurements. Experiments on the fastMRI knee dataset demonstrate that ZADS consistently outperforms both traditional compressed sensing and recent diffusion-based methods, showcasing its ability to deliver high-fidelity reconstructions across varying noise schedules and acquisition settings.

title: Optimized Sparse Network Coverage via L1-norm Minimization
authors: Souvik Paul, Iván Alexander Morales Sandoval, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas de Abreu
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: The selection of nodes that can serve as cluster heads, local sinks and gateways is a critical challenge in distributed sensor and communication networks. This paper presents a novel framework for identifying a minimal set of nexus nodes to ensure full network coverage while minimizing cost. By formulating the problem as a convex relaxation of the NP-hard set cover problem, we integrate the graph theoretic centrality measures of node degree and betweenness centrality into a cost function optimized via a relaxed $\ell_{1}$-norm minimization. The proposed approach is applicable to static and dynamic network scenarios and does not require location or distance estimation. Through simulations across various graph models and dynamic conditions, it is shown that the method achieves faster execution times (lower complexity) and competitive sparsity compared to classical greedy and genetic algorithms (GA), offering a robust, distributed, and cost-efficient node selection solution.

title: PPO-based Computing Task Offloading in Energy-Efficient Low-Altitude Networks
authors: Yuxin Wang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: In this paper, we aim to optimize task offloading, spectrum, and computing resource allocation in low-altitude networks. To improve the utilization of computing resources, we propose a three-mode computing framework, where tasks can be processed on users, individual unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), or offloaded to UAV swarms for collaborative processing. Furthermore, we formulate an optimization problem with the objective of minimizing system energy consumption while satisfying the task delay requirement and resource constraints. Due to the coupling of resources, it is difficult to solve the problem via a traditional optimization scheme. Therefore, we model this problem as a Markov decision process and propose a proximal policy optimization-based task offloading and resource allocation (PTORA) scheme that jointly optimizes task offloading decisions and the allocation of spectrum and computing resources. The proposed scheme leverages generalized advantage estimation to stabilize training and improve learning efficiency. Simulation results demonstrate that the PTORA scheme can significantly reduce energy consumption compared with state-of-the-art benchmarks.

title: Toward Safer Roads: A Low-Cost Intelligent Pothole Detection System for Smart Cities
authors: Fardin Kabir, Mardeni Roslee, Farman Ali, Yasir Ullah, Istiyak Amin Santo, Fahmid Kabir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Potholes represent a significant threat to urban road safety because they damage vehicles and endanger drivers. The research develops a small-scale IoT pothole detection system which uses an ultrasonic sensor and GPS module and Sony Spresense microcontroller with an integrated camera for live road surface inspection. The system automatically records geotagged images of potholes that surpass depth limits while it saves time- stamped data to a microSD card for future Google Maps visualization. The proposed system operates independently from the cloud while using minimal power and allowing users to add new components to the system. The YOLO-based image analysis module performs pixel-distance calibration to estimate pothole dimensions during post-processing for intelligent severity assessment. The system performed well during field tests by detecting potholes and mapping their GPS locations with acceptable precision. The system enables large-scale implementation for both road maintenance and smart city infrastructure management through its sensing-vision combination.

title: Outphasing Power Amplifiers: Overview and Comparison of Wilkinson and Chireix Combiners
authors: Nizar Brahim, Noureddine Boulejfene, Abdelkader Mami
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: In contemporary wireless communication systems, achieving both high efficiency and linearity in power amplifiers (PAs) remains a fundamental design challenge. These two performance metrics are often in conflict, requiring careful trade-offs. Extensive research has resulted in a broad range of PA architectures aimed at overcoming this efficiency–linearity dilemma. Among the promising techniques is the outphasing approach, also referred to as Linear Amplification using Nonlinear Components (LINC), which has the potential to deliver high efficiency while maintaining satisfactory linearity.

title: HCI Approaches to Reducing Cognitive Load in Cross-Device Peripheral Management
authors: Peijin Du, Minzhou Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Peripheral devices such as keyboards and mice are essential to gaming, creative, and productivity workflows, yet existing configuration tools remain fragmented and vendor-specific. Traditional driver-based software requires multiple installations, lacks consistent cross-platform support, and provides little visualization or accessibility, creating barriers for both novice and advanced users. This work introduces a new paradigm for peripheral management: a browser-based, installation-free platform that situates configuration within broader human–computer interaction (HCI) principles. Our prototype, F-Sync, reduces technical friction while integrating information visualization to lower cognitive load through immediate feedback, social computing to support collaborative configuration sharing and collective knowledge building, and IoT concepts to ensure seamless continuity of personalized setups across multiple devices and contexts.A controlled experiment with 20 participants demonstrated that features supported by the interactive visualization and community sharing achieved higher ratings in perceived usefulness and adoption intention, and while advanced keys and macro recording improved learning burden through visual feedback, it still involved a learning curve. Findings demonstrate how real-time feedback, simplified workflows, and community ecosystems can reduce cognitive load, accelerate adoption, and broaden accessibility. Beyond peripheral management, this research contributes design principles for visualization, crowdsourcing, and cross-device interaction that inform future directions in human–computer interaction.

title: Enhancing Electric Vehicle Performance Through Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Energy and Thermal Management
authors: Abdelaziz Sahbani
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: As the complexity of electric vehicles increases, effective control of energy usage and thermal management will be very important to increase efficiency and battery life. In EVs, the interconnected systems pose challenges for traditional control approaches and reinforcement learning with one single agent. The present work suggests a cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning structure in which two agents share control responsibilities, one for torque and one for battery temperature.It is the idea of having a shared reward to promote coordinated behavior in the absence of direct communication, while each agent makes use of relevant information on its own . The system as a whole is more efficient, scalable and flexible than learning in a single agent. After training the system through MAPPO and MADDPG algorithms, the trained agents are evaluated again on standard driving cycles UDDS and NEDC. The obtained results suggest that a cooperative multi-agent approach leads to better results in terms of energy efficiency, temperature control, and stability, finding that such a methodology be adopted in practice for EV charging.

title: Forestpest-YOLO: A High-Performance Detection Framework for Small Forestry Pests
authors: Aoduo Li, Peikai Lin, Jiancheng Li, Zhen Zhang, Shiting Wu, Zexiao Liang, Zhifa Jiang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Detecting agricultural pests in complex forestry environments using remote sensing imagery is fundamental for ecological preservation, yet it is severely hampered by practical challenges. Targets are often minuscule, heavily occluded, and visually similar to the cluttered background, causing conventional object detection models to falter due to the loss of fine-grained features and an inability to handle extreme data imbalance. To overcome these obstacles, this paper introduces Forestpest-YOLO, a detection framework meticulously optimized for the nuances of forestry remote sensing. Building upon the YOLOv8 architecture, our framework introduces a synergistic trio of innovations. We first integrate a lossless downsampling module, SPD-Conv, to ensure that critical high-resolution details of small targets are preserved throughout the network. This is complemented by a novel cross-stage feature fusion block, CSPOK, which dynamically enhances multi-scale feature representation while suppressing background noise. Finally, we employ VarifocalLoss to refine the training objective, compelling the model to focus on high-quality and hard-to-classify samples. Extensive experiments on our challenging, self-constructed ForestPest dataset demonstrate that Forestpest-YOLO achieves state-of-the-art performance, showing marked improvements in detecting small, occluded pests and significantly outperforming established baseline models.

title: Joint Active and Passive Beamforming Design for Multi-Stream MIMO RIS Systems: A Tensor-Based Approach
authors: Bruno Sokal, André L. F. de Almeida, Martin Haardt
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: In this paper, we propose two tensor-based algorithms for jointly designing active and passive beamformers in RIS-assisted multi-stream MIMO systems, by modeling the composite channel as a third-order tensor. The first method, based on the truncated higher-order singular value decomposition (T-HOSVD), provides a closed-form solution that utilizes tensor unfoldings, rendering it particularly suitable for low-rank scenarios. The second, tensor alternating optimization (TAO), iteratively refines the design by solving three subproblems, initialized with T-HOSVD. Simulations show that both methods significantly outperform existing approaches. While TAO achieves the highest spectral efficiency (SE), T-HOSVD offers a favorable performance-complexity trade-off, making it attractive for practical deployment.

title: Robust Synchronization of a Novel Four-Dimensional Hyperchaotic System via Adaptive Sliding Mode Control
authors: Amal Nasser, Mohamed Mchiri
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This paper deals with the design and analysis of a novel four-dimensional hyperchaotic system and the development of a robust adaptive sliding mode control strategy to achieve finite-time synchronization under parametric uncertainties and external disturbances. The proposed hyperchaotic system exhibits complex nonlinear dynamics with two positive Lyapunov exponents, confirming its hyperchaotic nature and making it suitable for secure communication applications. An adaptive sliding mode controller is designed based on integral sliding surfaces to guarantee robust synchronization between master and slave systems. The controller incorporates online parameter estimation to handle unknown system parameters, while the sliding mode approach ensures robustness against bounded external perturbations. Lyapunov stability analysis rigorously proves finite-time convergence of synchronization errors and parameter estimation errors. The theoretical results are validated through numerical simulations, demonstrating rapid error convergence and strong robustness. Finally, the practical utility of the synchronization scheme is illustrated through a chaotic masking application for secure signal transmission, where a sinusoidal signal is successfully masked and recovered using the synchronized hyperchaotic systems.

title: A Deep Learning based Eco-Driving Strategy for Connected and Automated Electric Vehicles with Driver Preferences
authors: Changxin Wan, Ziyan Zhang, Peng Hao, Kanok Boriboonsomsin, Matthew J. Barth, Yongkang Liu, Seyhan Ucar, Guoyuan Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE Forum for Innovative Sustainable Transportation Systems (FISTS)
abstract: Eco-driving strategy for connected and automated electric vehicles (CAEVs) shows great potential to improve traffic efficiency and reduce energy consumption. However, a fixed time-to- energy weight coefficient was usually assumed in existing studies, which often neglect considering different driver preferences (i.e., energy or time optimal). To address this problem, this paper proposes a deep learning based eco-driving strategy to predict the optimal acceleration while accounting for driver preferences. First, the eco-driving for CAEVs is formulated as a constrained optimization problem, and the optimal trajectory dataset is generated by the Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) method with nonlinear programming (NLP) solvers. Second, a novel imitation learning (IL) framework with a scenario encoder is proposed for mapping the optimal trajectory, and physical knowledge is fused to constrain the infeasible action of IL in rare cases. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method provides an accurate approximation of the optimal solution with different driver preferences. Furthermore, interpretability analysis is employed to reveal the causal relationships between network inputs and outputs. The code is provided by the Github repository: https://github.com/cwan-ucr/MLTPA-DP.git.

title: Predictive system for pricing used vehicles in Lima based on RestNet and XGBOOST
authors: Ysrael Nuñez, Pedro Grados, Roberto Montero
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: The used car market faces multiple challenges in price prediction, especially in unstable economic environments, where factors such as supply, demand, and market fluctuations complicate the valuation process. This study proposes an advanced predictive system that integrates machine learning models, using ResNet for vehicle image analysis and XGBoost for processing structured data such as age, mileage, model, and other vehicle characteristics. The main objective of this research is to improve the accuracy of used car price prediction, while also considering economic variations that impact market valuation. By validating this model with specific data from the Peruvian market, the aim is to offer a robust tool that allows sellers and buyers to make more informed decisions. This innovative approach would contribute to a more transparent and objective evaluation of used vehicles, benefiting both buyers and sellers in the buying and selling process.

title: Latent Diffusion Based Adaptive Image Representation for Multidimensional MRI
authors: Ruiyang Zhao, Jiachen Tu, Fan Lam
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: We present a new framework for representing and reconstructing multidimensional MR images via adaptive, feature-based representations. Specifically, we propose a disentangled representation model that separates different types of image features, such as geometry and contrast, into distinct lowdimensional latent spaces, reducing the degrees-of-freedom for multidimensional images and enabling flexible constraints on different latents/features. A latent diffusion model was introduced to capture the variations of these disentangled latents, providing feature-level generative priors for regularized reconstruction. New formulations and algorithms were developed to integrate the pretrained representation with task-specific adaptation for reconstruction from undersampled or noisy measurements, without task-specific training. We demonstrated effective disentanglement of geometry and contrast features in multi-contrast images, and its utility in different multidimensional MR applications, i.e., accelerated MR parameter mapping and self-supervised multicontrast denoising.

title: Modeling the Behavior and Performance of 5G Heterogeneous MidBand Networks in Urban Tripoli
authors: Sara Fkini, Amer R. Zerek
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: The deployment of fifth generation (5G) heterogeneous networks (HetNets) in dense urban areas such as Tripoli presents unique challenges related to user density, irregular building geometry, and midband signal propagation. This study develops a high-fidelity system-level simulation of a two-tier 5G HetNet operating at 3.5 GHz, integrating macro and small cells within a digital geographic model of Tripoli. Key performance indicators such as Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR), network throughput, and spectral efficiency were analyzed under 3GPP Urban Macrocell (UMa) and Urban Microcell (UMi) path loss conditions using proportional fair scheduling. The results indicate that dense small-cell deployment enhances capacity and coverage but introduces co-channel interference that constrains uniform service quality. The modeled network achieved an average SINR of 4.53 dB, a mean user throughput of 84.5 Mbps, and a median spectral efficiency of 2.48 bps/Hz. These findings establish a realistic performance baseline for midband 5G HetNets in urban Tripoli and underscore the need for advanced interference coordination in future network optimization.

title: Edge-Cloud Collaborative Fault Classification of Mechanical Equipment Using Lightweight Deep Learning Models
authors: Xinwei Wang, Jinjiang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: In the context of the intelligent transformation of the equipment manufacturing industry, traditional mechanical equipment condition monitoring methods based on deep learning often rely on cloud or server-side deployment, which suffers from issues such as high data transmission latency, strong dependence on network bandwidth, and inability to meet the requirements of low-latency application scenarios. To address these shortcomings, this paper proposes an edge-cloud collaborative fault classification method of mechanical equipment based on lightweight deep learning models. The model training and quantization are performed in the cloud using historical data, generating a lightweight model tailored for edge devices, which is then deployed for real-time edge inference and fault classification. At the edge, feature extraction and preprocessing based on the Hilbert transform are conducted prior to inference to enhance signal quality and reduce computational overhead. Using motor condition monitoring as an example, the results demonstrate that this method achieves an inference time of 0.846 seconds and a classification accuracy rate exceeding 97% in the edge computing environment, significantly reducing latency while maintaining high precision. Furthermore, under the proposed framework, various types of deep learning models demonstrate high classification accuracy and inference efficiency, further validating the method's versatility and engineering applicability.

title: Innovative MIMO Antenna Design with Enhanced Isolation for Wireless Communications in the 3-8 GHz
authors: Neila Kammoun, Meriem Drissi, Ferid Harabi, Ali Gharsallah
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This study introdces the design and simulation of a compact MIMO antenna system intended to operate across the 3–8 GHz frequency band. The antenna is intended for use in emerging wireless communication systems, including 5G sub-6 GHz C-band communication, and industrial IoT applications. The antenna design features two slotted circular patch elements arranged orthogonally, along with a decoupling mechanism that includes a T-shaped parasitic element and a defected grounded structure (DGS). This configuration enhances the isolation between the ports to over 20 dB across the entire frequency band. The design, implemented on an inexpensive FR4 substrate, offers wide bandwidth, high gain, low ECC (<0.02), and excellent diversity performance.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of a Full-Stack Cyber Attack-Defense Simulation Platform
authors: Jianxin Li, Jia Chao, Shih-Pang Tseng
abstract: A full-stack cyber attack-defense simulation platform is designed and implemented, integrating cloud services, KVM, visual analytics, and other advanced technologies. In accordance with teaching needs, user roles are divided into three types: students, teachers, and administrators. This platform aims to enhance the teaching effectiveness of network penetration testing practical training courses in higher vocational colleges. It addresses the deficiencies in current training approaches, such as high equipment costs, insufficient flexibility in training scenarios, and high risks associated with critical operations. By providing an efficient, secure, and controllable training environment, the platform helps improve students' learning interest, practical skills, and innovation awareness.

title: A Low-complexity Programmable Ultrasound Stimulation System: Design and Safety Evaluation
authors: Xuanjie Ye, Meimei Guo, Tianyi Wang, Sujie Wang, Jingjia Yuan, Tao Tan, Wenhua Shen, Li Huang, Fo Hu, Yu Sun
abstract: Accurate control, monitoring of acoustic power, and flexible waveform generation are essential for safe and reproducible transcranial focused ultrasound (tFUS) neuromodulation, which is not comprehensively supported by existing benchtop platforms. This work presents a low-complexity and programmable tFUS stimulation system. The system integrates a direct digital synthesis module, a DAC-controlled programmable DC-DC supply, a full-bridge driver, and an impedance matching network to achieve flexible waveform generation and efficient transducer excitation. Acoustic power is monitored using a nonuniform discrete Fourier transform method at the driving frequency. Direct amplitude regulation enables highly linear pressure control up to 4.85 MPa. Impedance matching raised the maximum peak-to-peak excitation voltage from 86 V to 206 V (×2.4) and reduced total harmonic distortion (THD) by 19.19 dB. The power monitor achieves <5% error for outputs above 3 W. In vivo safety was evaluated in mice using both acute (single 20-min exposure) and chronic (21-day, 20-min/day) protocols. Four stimulation groups at $\mathrm{I_{SPPA}}$ = 40 W/cm2 with duty cycles from 1.8% to 14.4% ($\mathrm{I_{SPTA}}$ = 0.72-5.76 W/cm2) were compared with sham and controls. Behavioral outcomes and histological analysis revealed no abnormalities under these conditions. The $\mathrm{I_{SPTA}}$ range corresponds to one to eight times the FDA guideline limit, thereby encompassing and extending typical safety margins in neuromodulation studies. These results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed platform, with validation at both the circuit level and through preclinical safety studies.

title: EMTR-Based Branch Identification for Accurate and Fast Fault Location
authors: Gayanjali Pathirage, Cassidy Flynn, Reza Razzaghi, Lachlan Andrew
abstract: Electromagnetic Time Reversal (EMTR) has been shown as a promising technique for fault localization in distribution networks. However, its application to larger networks is constrained by the need for finely spaced guessed fault points required in the back-propagation stage of the EMTR process. The need to assess numerous guessed locations can make the fault location process computationally expensive, and missing the matched fault point can lead to significant localization errors. This article proposes a novel EMTR-based method that pre-identifies the fault zone before pinpointing the fault location, by strategically placing guessed fault points solely at the network’s leaf nodes. Extensive simulations across networks of varying scales demonstrate that this approach substantially reduces the network search area while enhancing localization accuracy when the matched point is missed.

title: A Novel Trench IGBT With Shielding Hole Path for Low EMI Noise
authors: Tongyang Wang, Zehong Li, Yishang Zhao, Ziming Xia, Yige Zheng, Zhaoqing Jin, Wei Li, Xiaohan Liu
abstract: A novel trench insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) with shielding hole path (SHP) is proposed and experimentally demonstrated. During turn-on, the SHP shields the gate–collector coupling and provides a hole path (HP) near the main current path. A hole barrier is introduced near the gate trench to shield hole accumulation and form a hole depletion region. A hole well is introduced near the split-gate (SG) trench to enhance hole extraction through the SHP. Consequently, the displacement current that charges the gate electrode is effectively suppressed through the reduction of both the effective ${C}_{\text {GC}}$ for holes and $\textit {dV}_{\text {acc}}$ /dt. The experimental results show that, compared to the floating P-Region IGBT (FP-IGBT), the SHP-IGBT achieves a 49.0% lower $\textit {dI}_{\text {C}}$ /dt at the same ${R}_{\text {G}}$ and a 54.7% lower $\textit {dV}_{\text {KA}}$ / $\textit {dt}_{\max }$ at the same ${E}_{\text {on}}$ , reducing electromagnetic interference (EMI) noise without degrading other electrical parameters. In addition, a refined displacement current model incorporating the effective ${C}_{\text {GC}}$ for holes is proposed.

title: An Approach to Electron Optical System Design for Dual-Pencil-Beam Traveling Wave Tubes
authors: Dongdong Jia, Hairong Yin, Pengcheng Yin, Wuyang Fan, Jian Zhang, Yue Ouyang, Jinchi Cai, Jin Xu, Lingna Yue, Yong Xu, Wenxiang Wang, Yanyu Wei
abstract: Achieving a high compression factor and stable long-distance transmission of electron beams presents significant challenges for developing multibeam devices, especially those that employ multiple pencil beams. Uniform magnetic (UM) field focusing structures, although viable, are often limited by their bulkiness and weight. This article presents a novel approach for designing dual-beam electron guns with significantly enhanced electron beam compression. The proposed methodology, implemented in W-band overmoded traveling wave tubes (TWTs), demonstrates exceptional performance, achieving a remarkable beam compression factor of 57 at a total current of 0.2 A (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 0.1$</tex-math> </inline-formula> A). In addition, a compact periodic cusped magnetic structure with a notch is proposed to effectively confine dual pencil beams. Co-simulation results of the electron optical system demonstrate a transmission efficiency of 99.2% over a distance of 100 mm.

title: A Complete Solution to Generalized Relative Pose Estimation from Affine Correspondences
authors: Banglei Guan, Ji Zhao, Laurent Kneip
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: In recent years, affine correspondences (ACs) have emerged as widely adopted alternative to point correspondences (PCs) in geometric problems in computer vision. An AC is composed of a PC across two different views plus an affine transformation between the small patches around this PC. Prior studies have shown that a single affine correspondence (AC) generally yields three independent constraints for estimating relative pose. This work addresses relative pose estimation in multi-perspective camera systems, a relevant problem given their prevalence in modern technologies such as autonomous vehicles and augmented reality. More specifically, we introduce the first comprehensive suite of minimal solvers for 6DoF relative pose estimation across multiple cameras using only two ACs, which is notably valuable for robust model fitting scenarios. We analyze all possible configurations of two ACs in two views, and present minimal solvers covering all identified minimal cases. We make use of the hidden variable technique to eliminate the translation parameters, and represent rotation using either Cayley parameters or quaternions. We furthermore introduce novel constraints on the generalized relative pose problem that are beneficial in deriving more compact solvers with fewer solutions. Comprehensive experiments on synthetic and real-world data show that the proposed affine correspondence-based solvers are highly effective and computationally efficient.

title: SELongVLM: Empowering Long Video Language Models with Self-Corrective Clip Selection
authors: Kecheng Zhang, Zongxin Yang, Mingfei Han, Yunzhi Zhuge, Haihong Hao, Changlin Li, Zhihui Li, Xiaojun Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have enabled impressive progress in visual–language reasoning, yet long-video understanding remains a formidable challenge due to the need for coherent reasoning over ultra-long spatiotemporal dependencies. Existing methods struggle with the vast candidate space for relevant information in long videos, often failing to distinguish meaningful events from redundant content. We identify two critical and previously under-explored issues: absolute redundancy, where static visual content inflates token counts without adding narrative value, and relative redundancy, where task-irrelevant segments introduce noise that impairs reasoning. Compounding these issues is the weak spatiotemporal modeling in current MLLMs, which limits their ability to capture complex event dynamics. To address these multifaceted challenges, we introduce SELongVLM, a dynamically lenient-to-stringent selection long video language model. SELongVLM integrates two coordinated branches: a Residual Token Pruner (RTP) that removes repetitive background tokens via inter-frame residual modeling thus mitigating absolute redundancy while preserving motion cues, and a Semantic-aware Self-Correction Selector (SCSelector) that progressively refines query-relevant clip selection without frame-level annotations to reduce relative redundancy, guided by a stringent-to-lenient self-correcting mechanism during optimization. To ensure causal continuity and bolster spatiotemporal reasoning across disjoint clips, the framework further incorporates an action-aware operation for intra-clip dynamics and a temporal memory for cross-clip context, enabling robust spatiotemporal inference on long videos. Extensive experiments across eight benchmarks demonstrate that SELongVLM markedly outperforms existing models on both general and specialized long-video tasks. Specifically, it achieves 65.5% on VideoMME and 69.8% on MLVU for general benchmarks, and delivers strong performance on four specialized benchmarks - for example, 39.2% on TOMATO for fine-grained temporal reasoning and 69.2% on EventBench for event-level understanding.

title: Flexible and Stretchable Optical Waveguide Tactile Sensor for 3-Axial Force Sensing
authors: Yue Li, Wenlong Gaozhang, Jian Hu, Gongyu Zhang, Prokar Dasgupta, Sebastien Ourselin, Hongbin Liu
abstract: Tactile sensing is essential for precise manipulation and ensuring safety by providing real-time feedback on external forces and interactions. However, conventional tactile sensors struggle to maintain flexibility, accuracy, and reliability when applied to complex or flexible surfaces. To address these limitations, we present a novel, fully flexible optical waveguide sensor capable of 3-axial force sensing by detecting variations in light intensity within intersecting optical channels. This work includes a comprehensive theoretical analysis and modeling of optical waveguide mechanisms, focusing on intrinsic, extrinsic, and intersection losses, thereby establishing a foundation for optimizing sensor design and minimizing these losses. The sensor's performance was validated through experiments involving various deformation modes, including stretching, bending, and pressing, achieving a resolution of 0.0073 N and a hysteresis of 4.76%. To model the relationship between applied forces and light intensity loss, we employed an artificial neural network, which achieved an error margin of 0.10058 N (R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> = 0.98472) in force prediction. The combination of high performance, flexibility, and accuracy makes this sensor a promising solution for tactile sensing in advanced applications such as flexible systems and soft robotics.

title: IEEE Transactions on Computers
authors: N/A

title: Dynamic Reserve Sizing with Statistically Feasible Guarantees: An Uncertainty-Adaptive Robust Optimization Framework
authors: Chen Ling, Chenye Wu
abstract: The integration of intermittent renewable energy and increasing load volatility necessitates adaptive reserve sizing frameworks to balance grid reliability and economic efficiency. Static reserve allocation methods struggle with dynamic uncertainty, risking high costs or compromised security. This study introduces a probabilistic reserve sizing framework with three innovations: 1) a dynamic architecture for economic dispatch and Unit Commitment (UC) employing a unified joint chance constraint to manage system-wide, temporally-coupled risks under uncertainty; 2) a dual-stage statistically feasible robust optimization (RO) approach employing a novel unified dimension-collapsing map, enabling tractable learning-calibrated uncertainty set construction with dimension-free out-of-sample reliability guarantees; and 3) a Learning-to-Optimize acceleration strategy that transforms the robust UC into a tractable linear program, enabling scalability. Validation on standard test systems up to 118 buses demonstrates the framework's ability to dynamically adjust reserves through uncertainty-adaptive quantification. The solution reconciles statistical feasibility with computational efficiency, achieving scalable, cost-effective, and reliable operations. By integrating statistically feasible RO with machine learning acceleration, this framework provides a practical approach to managing uncertainty and ensuring economic and operational balance in power systems with high renewable penetration.
