title: Employee’s Work Motivation in The Portman Ritz-Carlton Shanghai from Herzberg’s Two-Factor Theory
authors: Na-Na Li, Bin-Wen Yu, Si-Si Zhang
abstract: Employees’ work enthusiasm and job satisfaction directly determine service quality and customer experience, thereby affecting the sustainable development of hotels. Employee motivation is an important means to enhance employee work enthusiasm. Guided by Herzberg’s motivation-hygiene theory, this study examines the employee motivation of the Portman Ritz-Carlton Shanghai. The findings reveal that the hotel performs excellently in the motivation factors of work recognition and rewards and the hygiene factors of management and fairness and interpersonal relationships and support. There remain shortcomings in the hygiene factors of work environment and compensation and benefits, and the motivational factors of training and team building. Particularly, participation and empowerment fail to effectively stimulate employees’ intrinsic enthusiasm. Based on these findings, this paper proposes improvement strategies, including optimizing the work environment, improving the compensation and benefits system, establishing training and team-building mechanisms, increasing participation and empowerment to increase employees’ work passion.

title: Respiratory Disease Classification Using Audio Data: A Multi-Model Machine Learning Approach
authors: Heng Li, Weicong Feng, Liang Zhao, Feng Gu
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Respiratory disease presents major global health challenges, and its early detection could save numerous lives, prompting the demand for accurate and non-intrusive diagnostic methods. This study presents a multi-model machine learning framework for classifying respiratory diseases (COPD, Bronchiolitis, Pneumonia, URTI, and Healthy) using audio data from the Respiratory Sound Database. We extract Mel frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) and apply data augmentation to address class imbalance. We propose a novel hybrid model, ConvMambda, and compare its performance against other popular classification models, including 1D CNN, random forest, SVM, and LSTM. The ConvMamba model achieves the highest test accuracy (90.77%) and macro F1-score (0.8932), outperforming 1D-CNN (90% accuracy) and random forest (82% accuracy), SVM (68% accuracy), and LSTM (68% accuracy). We analyze the performance across classes, highlighting challenges with minority classes (Bronchiolitis, URTI) and propose strategies to mitigate imbalance. Our findings demonstrate the potential of audio-based diagnostics, with the ConvMamba model offering the superior performance for respiratory diseases classification.

title: Codebook-Based Port Selection and Combining for CSI-Free Uplink Fluid Antenna Multiple Access
authors: Chenguang Rao, Kai-Kit Wong, Sai Xu, Xusheng Zhu, Yangyang Zhang, Chan-Byoung Chae
abstract: Fluid antenna multiple access (FAMA) has recently emerged as a simple, promising scheme for large-scale multiuser connectivity, offering strong scalability with low implementation complexity. Nevertheless, most existing FAMA studies focus on downlink transmission under perfect channel state information (CSI) at the receiver side, while the uplink counterpart remains largely unexplored. This paper proposes a novel codebook-based port selection and combining (CPSC) FAMA framework for the uplink communications without CSI at the base station (BS). In the proposed scheme, a predefined codebook is designed and broadcast by the BS. Each user equipment (UE) employs a fluid antenna, acquires its local CSI and independently chooses the most suitable codeword, activates the corresponding fluid antenna ports, and determines the combining weights to achieve a two-way match between the selected codeword and the instantaneous effective channel. The BS then separates the superimposed user signals through codebook-guided projection operations without requiring global CSI or multiuser joint optimization. To handle potential codeword collisions, three lightweight scheduling strategies are introduced, offering flexible trade-offs between signaling overhead and collision avoidance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed CPSC-FAMA approach achieves substantially higher rates than fixed-antenna systems while maintaining low complexity. Moreover, the results confirm that amortizing the optimization cost over the UEs effectively reduces the BS processing burden and enhances scalability, making the proposed scheme a strong candidate for future sixth-generation (6G) networks.

title: Towards Enhanced QoS in MEC Network Slicing: A Hierarchical Closed-loop Approach to Efficient Resource Allocation
authors: Juntao Xu, Xinggang Fan, Changfu Xu, Haodong Zou, Haiyang Huang, Yuzhu Liang
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Computers and Artificial Intelligence Technology (CAIT)
abstract: Efficient resource allocation in 5G/6G-enabled Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) environments is crucial for ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) across heterogeneous network slices. However, existing approaches often can not make full use of resource utilization, resulting in low service success rates under heterogeneous network conditions. To address this issue, we first formalize the resource allocation problem of MEC network slicing and demonstrate its NP-hardness through a reduction to the multi-dimensional $\mathbf{0 - 1}$ knapsack problem. We then propose HCLO, a Hierarchical Closed-Loop Optimization approach, where an enhanced genetic algorithm based on adaptive particle swarm optimization is designed to generate high-quality resource allocation decisions. Moreover, a multi-dimensional weighted evaluation model is proposed to translate diverse service requirements into quantifiable QoS constraints accurately. As a result, our HCLO approach enables a closed-loop collaboration way that facilitates the joint optimization of caching, bandwidth, and computational resources. Experimental results show that the proposed HCLO approach significantly outperforms baseline approaches by enhancing the resource utilization of $\mathbf{3. 6 8 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 6. 5 6 \%}$ and the user satisfaction of $\mathbf{1 1. 0 8 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{4 8. 7 2 \%}$ in heterogeneous MEC network slicing environments.

title: A System for UAV Obstacle Avoidance Control via Haptic Interface and Optical Tracking
authors: Kaiyuan Lu, Wenbin Liu, Mikhail Svinin, Kaiyuan Wang, Igor Goncharenko
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: This paper outlines the design and implementation of a full haptic feedback system developed to accomplish real-time teleoperation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). The system allows the teleoperation of a drone within indoor environments rich with obstacles by using only haptic feedback and without visual feedback. Our system efficiently encompasses using Unity as the processing and visualization center, ROS2 as the communication and robot control system, Geomagic Touch as the haptic device and controller, and OptiTrack to provide real-time position tracking of both the UAV and surrounding obstacles. The force feedback is dynamically calculated within Unity based on the distance to obstacles and is transmitted to the Geomagic device to provide better intuitive spatial perception. Experimental results demonstrate the ability of teleoperators to safely traverse through obstacles without visual cues, demonstrating the system's responsiveness, low-latency system design, and precision of the real-time feedback. The developed framework provides a modular and efficient architecture to achieve intuitive teleoperation of a drone within confined environments.

title: A Novel Hybrid Framework for Image Denoising under Mixed Impulsive and Gaussian Noise
authors: Bogdan Smolka, Damian Kusnik, Milena Smolka
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: We present a denoising framework tailored for color images degraded by mixed Gaussian and impulsive noise. The process begins with bilateral filtering to suppress Gaussian noise while preserving pixels corrupted by impulsive noise process. Impulses are then detected using a novel method combined with k-means classification and are restored via an efficient inpainting algorithm based on Laplacian interpolation. To further enhance image quality, a non-local means filter is applied to eliminate residual noise and recover fine textures. The proposed method integrates edge-preserving smoothing, structure-aware impulsive noise removal through inpainting, and non-local smoothing, with adaptive parameter tuning based on noise levels and image content. Experimental results show that the proposed hybrid approach delivers results that match or surpass those of current state-of-the-art denoising techniques.

title: A Multiscale Geometric Method for Capturing Relational Topic Alignment
authors: Conrad D. Hougen, Karl T. Pazdernik, Alfred O. Hero
publish: 2025 IEEE 10th International Workshop on Computational Advances in Multi-Sensor Adaptive Processing (CAMSAP)
abstract: Interpretable topic modeling is essential for tracking how research interests evolve within co-author communities. In scientific corpora, where novelty is prized, identifying underrepresented niche topics is particularly important. However, contemporary models built from dense transformer embeddings tend to miss rare topics and therefore also fail to capture smooth temporal alignment. We propose a geometric method that integrates multimodal text and co-author network data, using Hellinger distances and Ward’s linkage to construct a hierarchical topic dendrogram. This approach captures both local and global structure, supporting multiscale learning across semantic and temporal dimensions. Our method effectively identifies rare-topic structure and visualizes smooth topic drift over time. Experiments highlight the strength of interpretable bag-ofwords models when paired with principled geometric alignment.

title: Poles of Green Function for Newtonian Particle in an Infinite Plane Wave
authors: Huber Nieto-Chaupis
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: Commonly the well-known Newton’s 2th law establishes that any massive body experience an acceleration while its momentum has been varied with respect to time. This is fundamental to support the theorem of work and energy. The purpose of this paper is twofold: (i) In one hand to derive the associate Green’s function directly the Newton’s 2th law in a closed form, and (ii) extend the case of charged particle when interacts to an external electromagnetic field modeled through a infinite plane wave. To accomplish this it has been used the well-known methodologies based in the usage of Green’s functions. This has allowed to interpreted the dynamical manifestation of massive and charged particles under the influence of external fields, but in scenario dictated by classical mechanics. Some expressions related to quantum mechanics have spontaneously emerged.

title: AI for a Greener Planet: Revolutionizing Energy Systems for Climate Resilience
authors: Sahibzada Muhammad Ali, Aqib Hassan, Mansoor Iqbal, Khalid Munir, Muhammad Naveed, Umer Saddique
publish: 2025 International Conference on Engineering and Emerging Technologies (ICEET)
abstract: The global energy sector is at a tipping point, driven by the dual imperatives of climate change mitigation and the transition to sustainable energy. This paper introduces a comprehensive AI-driven framework that revolutionizes modern energy systems through climate-aware forecasting, real-time control, adaptive demand response, and distributed optimization. By combining deep learning, reinforcement learning, and edge intelligence, the proposed architecture significantly enhances resilience, efficiency, and decarbonization. Real-world inspired case studies—including LSTM-based solar prediction, MPC-RL hybrid grid regulation, DRL-based demand flexibility, and AI-enabled microgrid resilience—demonstrate up to 41% forecast improvement, 50% faster frequency recovery, and 36% emissions reduction. The paper concludes with a policy roadmap to scale AI across infrastructure, governance, and global collaboration, establishing AI as a cornerstone in climate-resilient energy futures.

title: Feasibility of Robotic-Assisted Echocardiography in Patients: Reducing Sonographer Strain without Compromising Image Quality
authors: Kumaradevan Punithakumar, Ahmed Ahmed, Moath Ghanem, Michelle Noga, Bernadette Foster, Pierre Boulanger, Harald Becher, Jonathan Windram
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Echocardiography remains a widely used imaging modality for the evaluation of cardiac structure and function. Despite its diagnostic value, conventional manual scanning techniques require sonographers to maintain repetitive postures and apply sustained pressure over extended periods, increasing the risk of work-related musculoskeletal disorders. In recent years, collaborative robots, or cobots, have emerged as a promising solution for applications requiring robots to operate safely alongside human operators. Modern cobot systems are often equipped with integrated force and torque sensors, enabling precise control of contact forces during patient scanning to ensure both safety and comfort. This study investigates the feasibility of a robotic-assisted echocardiography system in a clinical setting, focusing on its potential to reduce physical strain on sonographers while maintaining diagnostic image quality. A patient-based evaluation over 24 participants was conducted to assess system performance, force control accuracy, and image quality compared to conventional manual scanning. The findings aim to provide insights into the integration of robotic assistance in echocardiography workflows, with implications for improving operator ergonomics, patient safety, and imaging quality.

title: Exploring the Drivers of Smartphone Purchase Intentions in China’s Online Retail Market: Insights from JD.com
authors: Tiantian Yuan, Randong Pei, Shanqing Chen, Qimei Long
abstract: Smartphones have become an indispensable part of people’s daily life. As competition among smartphone brands intensifies, understanding the determinants of consumer purchase intention is crucial for enterprises. This study investigates the main factors influencing consumers’ purchase intention of smartphones on JD.com, focusing on consumers aged 18–30. A questionnaire survey was conducted, and 509 valid responses were collected. Using SPSS for data analysis, descriptive statistics, principal component analysis, correlation analysis, and regression analysis were applied to test the proposed hypotheses. The results indicate that brand effect, psychological factors, online reviews, and smartphone performance significantly and positively influence purchase intention. Among these, brand trust, product durability, and online review quantity show the strongest impact. The findings provide insights into consumer behavior and offer practical implications for smartphone manufacturers to enhance competitiveness and marketing strategy.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Algorithms for Predictive Modeling of Truck Cycle Time in Open-Pit Mining Operations
authors: Milka C I Madahana, Nkwane Phahlamohlaka, John E. D. Ekoru
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI)
abstract: Truck cycle time is a critical performance indicator in open-pit mining operations, directly impacting productivity, fleet management efficiency, and operational costs. Accurate real time prediction of truck cycle time allows for dynamic dispatching and scheduling optimization, which is essential for maximizing material handling throughput. This study presents a comprehensive comparative evaluation of eight distinct machine learning algorithms for the predictive modelling of truck cycle time in a real-world open-pit mine environment. The investigation leverages a robust dataset of truck movement and operational parameters to train and validate the performance of the following models: Artificial Neural Network, Bayesian Network, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-Nearest Neighbors <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(k$</tex>-NN), Decision Tree, and Linear Regression. The models are rigorously assessed based on key performance metrics, including Root Mean Square Error, Mean Absolute Error, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score, to determine their accuracy and reliability in estimating future truck cycle time. Results indicate significant variability in predictive power across the algorithms, highlighting the necessity of an informed model selection process for this specific industrial application. Specifically, the Random Forest and Artificial Neural Network models generally demonstrated superior predictive performance due to their capacity to capture complex, non-linear relationships within the haulage data. Conversely, simpler linear models exhibited lower accuracy. The main contribution of this research paper is the provision of a clear methodology and empirical evidence for selecting the most appropriate machine learning algorithm, offering a crucial contribution towards developing highly efficient, data-driven truck dispatching and management systems that enhance overall mine profitability

title: A New Algebraic Criterion for Robust Stability and Control design of Switched TS Fuzzy Systems with Neutral Delays
authors: Sabrine Ben Tekaya, Ahmed Jaballi, Marwen Kermani, Anis Sakly, Ahmed El Hajjaji
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference on Signal, Control and Communication (SCC)
abstract: This study focuses on the stability and control synthesis of discrete-time uncertain switched systems, modeled within the Takagi Sugeno (TS) fuzzy framework and including neutral-type delays. Emphasis is placed on introducing novel algebraic criteria that move beyond conventional Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI) approaches. The proposed methodology leverages the characteristics of M-matrices alongside the l<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1,∞</inf> induced norm to derive robust algebraic stability conditions. The proposed results are validated via numerical examples.

title: Robust Two-Stage Deep Reinforcement Learning-based Connected Eco-Driving Strategy along Adaptive Signalized Corridors
authors: Haishan Liu, Peng Hao, Ziye Qin, Matthew Barth, Kanok Boriboonsomsin
publish: 2026 IEEE Forum for Innovative Sustainable Transportation Systems (FISTS)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust strategy for connected truck eco-driving along coordinated signalized corridors considering the variability of real-world actuated signal control. We introduce a two-stage deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithm designed to learn optimal segment-level travel time to efficiently traverse upcoming intersections, reducing total energy consumption without sacrificing mobility efficiency. The strategy consists of a high-level DRL agent that recommends a segment travel time recommendation based on observations of distances to the remaining intersections and time-in-cycle of the traffic signals at those remaining intersections. The agent employs the proximal policy optimization algorithm to learn the optimal strategy under dynamic traffic signal patterns. Given each segment travel time, dynamic programming is then applied to obtain an optimal speed profile. A vehicle energy consumption model estimates the energy consumption of the speed profile, which is provided as feedback to the DRL agent in order to encourage more energy-efficient decisions. The DRL agent learns to make efficient long-term and short-term decisions from different traffic signal patterns including coordination, phase extension, and phase skipping. We conducted simulation studies using real-world signal phase and timing data collected from an urban freight corridor of Atlantic Avenue in Long Beach, California. The results demonstrated that the proposed strategy is robust and adaptive to various signal patterns over multiple days. Compared to the intelligent driver model, the proposed strategy achieves an average of 15.03% energy savings with 8.12% reduction in travel time. The proposed strategy can be adapted for other types of vehicles and powertrains, by customizing the vehicle dynamics and energy consumption modules accordingly.

title: Robotic Vision on Unethical Behavior and Speech Recognition
authors: Shih-Pang Tseng, Jia-Hao Lu, Jhing-Fa Wang, Yi-Chang Chen, Fengying Li
abstract: As humanoid robots integrate into society, the ability to detect and respond to unethical conduct would be essential. In this paper, we propose a unified multi-modal framework combining YOLO-Track for behavior detection and Whisper with LLM-based classification for speech analysis. Our system targets 10 unethical behaviors and 10 speech types, achieving F1-scores of 92% and 91.2%, respectively, outperforming existing methods. This work advances ethical awareness in humanoid robotics.

title: A Collaborative Edge-Cloud Architecture for Low-Latency Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Human-Robot Interaction
authors: Hsin-Chun Tsai, Jhing-Fa Wang, Jie-Ming Liu
abstract: To enhance the utility and human-like nature of service robots, equipping them with the ability to perceive human emotions is essential for valuable Human-Robot Interaction (HRI). However, deploying computationally intensive Multimodal Emotion Recognition (MER) models on resource-constrained mobile platforms presents significant architectural challenges, namely network latency and server I/O bottlenecks. This paper introduces a novel collaborative edge-cloud architecture to overcome these hurdles. Our architecture integrates a low-latency WebSocket framework for real-time communication, an intelligent client-side Voice Activity Detection (VAD) to minimize unnecessary data transmission, and a fileless, in-memory server-side pipeline for rapid processing and multi-person tracking. This system successfully embodies a high-performance MER model onto a Temi robot, enabling it to recognize and respond to emotions in multi-person environments.

title: MULIC: Implicit Neural Coding Framework for Multi-Scene Light-Field
authors: Trung Hieu Pham, Eiji Kamioka, Phan Xuan Tan
abstract: Light-field images capture both spatial and angular information of a scene, enabling immersive visual experiences but resulting in extremely large data volumes that pose challenges for storage and transmission. To address this, we propose MULIC, a unified implicit neural representation (INR) framework for efficient light-field compression. Unlike conventional INR-based methods that require training a separate model for each scene, MULIC jointly encodes multiple light-field images within a single compact network. The model learns a continuous mapping from scene and angular indices to sub-aperture images, allowing shared parameters to effectively capture cross-scene redundancies. To achieve practical compression, MULIC integrates dynamic pruning, adaptive quantization-aware training, and explicit byte-level packing, ensuring that measured bitrates reflect true on-disk sizes. Experimental results on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that MULIC achieves high compression efficiency and reconstruction fidelity. In addition, the model supports real-time decoding and continuous novel view synthesis, providing a compact and scalable foundation for neural light-field representation.

title: A Smooth Time-Optimal Motion Control Strategy of Industrial Manipulator Subject to Hybrid Joint Constraints Synthesized by Low-Computational-Complexity Zeroing Neural Network
authors: Junpei Yang, Zhan Li
abstract: Time-optimal motion control (TOMC) poses a significant challenge in the industrial robotics domain, focusing on reducing the execution time of motion tasks while minimizing joint jitter for enhanced operational efficiency and safety. This article proposes a novel optimization framework subject to hybrid joint constraints that simultaneously achieves time optimality and smooth joint velocities by formulating an L<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{p}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-norm optimization model. The proposed TOMC optimization paradigm is solved by a low-computational-complexity zeroing neural network (LCCZNN) to obtain accurate and robust end-effector path tracking performance. The method effectively reduces oscillation and jitter in resolved joint velocity profiles, improving both TOMC feasibility and smoothness. Simulations and experiments on the UR3e and Franka Emika Panda robotic arm demonstrate that the proposed approach enhances trajectory tracking accuracy, allowing path tracking to be completed twice as fast as previous methods, while also enabling robust tracking against noise. This study provides a promising direction for developing robust, constraint-aware motion control algorithms for high-performance industrial robots.

title: Surviving Disruptions: Exploitation or Exploration for Data Value Creation
authors: Fanny-Eve Bordeleau
abstract: Enhancing a firm's data value creation when the firm undergoes supply chain disruptions is a significant challenge. Previous research indicates senior management attitudes, data capabilities, and organizational learning capabilities lead to data value creation. We investigate two Canadian medium-sized enterprises in the electronic manufacturing sector, which experienced significant disruptions around the COVID-19 pandemic. An interview was conducted with executives in 2017, and follow-ups were carried out in 2023 to understand the influence of their organizational learning approach on data value creation. We find exploitation capabilities safeguard a firm's ability to sustain operational and strategic data value creation during disruptions. Exploration capabilities facilitate an increase in strategic data value creation during the recovery but have less impact on operational data value creation. This shows the link between data capabilities and value creation may not be taken for granted during disruptions. Managers should adapt their organizational learning style to the current disruptions.

title:
Ultra-Wide-Bandwidth Edge Coupler with Large Mode Spot Size on Thin-Film LiTaO
                    <sub>3</sub>
authors: Yiqi Dai, Chijun Li, Tingan Li, An Pan, Xiao Wu, Shuai Yuan, Guanglian Cheng, Yuankang Huang, Siyu Lu, Qiyuan Li, Yang He, Cheng Zeng, Jinsong Xia
abstract: Thin-film lithium tantalate (TFLT) is an emerging electro-optic material with properties comparable to lithium niobate and promising applications in communications, sensing, and computing. With advances in wafer fabrication and low-loss dry-etched waveguides, TFLT has gained increasing attention. However, efficient fiber-to-chip coupling remains critical, especially for large-NA fibers that complicate mode conversion. Here, we demonstrate a TFLT large-mode-size edge coupler fabricated by standard semiconductor processes. Coupling to a high-NA fiber (MFD ∼6.5 μm) exhibits losses below 0.5 dB/facet in the O-band, below 0.37 dB/facet in the C-band, and 0.22 dB/facet at 1482 nm. The coupler maintains <1.3 dB/facet across 1200–2400 nm, with high misalignment tolerance and power stability.

title: Evolving Task-Agnostic Features from Task-Specific Priors for Multi-Task Dense Prediction
authors: Qi Li, Jiexin Luo, Wenqi Deng, Wenjie Yang, Fei Chen, Yuanlong Yu, Jia Pan, Shengfeng He, Wenxi Liu
abstract: Previous multi-task dense prediction methods based on a decoder-centric paradigm have achieved promising results. They primarily rely on task-agnostic features extracted from a pre-trained backbone, overlooking the importance of task-specific prior knowledge. In this paper, we propose a novel approach that integrates task priors into task-agnostic features to facilitate the development of a more refined task-specific representativeness. Specifically, we introduce a CNN-based task-prior awareness module to capture comprehensive task-specific priors. As the cornerstone of feature interaction, task-agnostic features (e.g., pretrained ViT tokens) recurrently absorb the extracted task priors, evolving into multi-task-informed features. In the meantime, multi-task-informed features enhance task-specific features in favor of dense prediction. Furthermore, to address the inherent incompatibility between ViT tokens and CNN-based priors, we incorporate multi-scale collaborative feature filters within the decoder that adaptively generate low-pass and high-pass kernels to modulate their collaboration. Extensive experiments on the PASCAL-Context and NYUD-v2 datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves comparable or even better performance than the existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Sparrow: Data-Efficient Video-LLM with Text-to-Image Augmentation
authors: Shukang Yin, Chaoyou Fu, Sirui Zhao, Chunjiang Ge, Yan Yang, Yuhan Dai, Yongdong Luo, Tong Xu, Caifeng Shan, Enhong Chen
abstract: Recent Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in vision understanding, largely attributed to scaling laws emphasizing larger models and datasets. However, the effectiveness of scaling with synthetic data remains underexplored. This work investigates video-LLM development from a data-centric perspective by fine-tuning pre-trained image-LLMs with video data. Our experiments reveal low learning efficiency when naively scaling video data, which we attribute to limited instruction diversity. To address this, we propose Sparrow, a data augmentation method that synthesizes “video” samples from text instruction data. By mixing these synthetic samples with video data, our approach achieves comparable or superior performance using significantly fewer training samples. Additionally, incorporating synthetic samples enhances long video understanding without requiring long video training data. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate our method's effectiveness and efficiency across multiple benchmarks.

title: Distributed Time-Varying Nash Equilibrium Searching for Nonlinear Placement of Multi-cluster Autonomous Aerial Vehicle Formations
authors: Tao Hu, Yang Chen, Yan-Jun Liu, Lei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the nonlinear placement problem for multi-cluster autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) formations. The AAVs in each cluster are positioned to achieve the target configuration while minimizing the sum of the squares Euclidean space distances between each center and its corresponding members of the cluster. The problem is reformulated as a time-varying noncooperative game. Firstly, considering the external disturbances of AAVs, a fast terminal sliding mode observer (FTSM) is developed to estimate the disturbance, and the convergence of the observer is proved by the Lyapunov stability theorem. Then, to search Nash equilibrium (NE) for the time-varying non-cooperative game problems, a distributed algorithm based on adaptive connectivity maintenance mechanism and gradient descent method is designed. The algorithm achieves asymptotic convergence for Nash equilibrium, which is proved by means of an iterative approach. Finally, the algorithm's effectiveness is demonstrated through a simulation example.

title: Impact of 5G Latency and Jitter on TAS Scheduling in a 5G-TSN Network: An Empirical Study
authors: Pablo Rodriguez-Martin, Oscar Adamuz-Hinojosa, Pablo Muñoz, Julia Caleya-Sanchez, Pablo Ameigeiras
abstract: Deterministic communications are essential to meet the stringent delay and jitter requirements of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) services. IIoT increasingly demands wide-area wireless mobility to support Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMR) and dynamic workflows. Integrating Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) with 5G private networks is emerging as a promising approach to fulfill these requirements. In this architecture, 5G provides wireless access for industrial devices, which connect to a TSN backbone that interfaces with the enterprise edge/cloud, where IIoT control and computing systems reside. TSN achieves bounded latency and low jitter using IEEE 802.1Qbv Time-Aware Shaper (TAS), which schedules the network traffic in precise time slots. However, the stochastic delay and jitter inherent in 5G disrupt TSN scheduling, requiring careful tuning of TAS parameters to maintain end-to-end determinism. This paper presents an empirical study evaluating the impact of 5G downlink delay and jitter on TAS scheduling using a testbed with TSN switches and a commercial 5G network. Results show that guaranteeing bounded latency and jitter requires careful setting of TAS transmission window offset between TSN switches based on the measured 5G delay bounded by a high order p-th percentile. Otherwise, excessive offset may cause additional delay or even a complete loss of determinism.

title: Investigation of Electrocatalysis for Electrical Field Based Anti-Interference Histamine Biosensor with Readout Circuit
authors: Wei-Cheng Lin, Tzu-Wei Chung, Yi-Huan Chang, Bing-Hong Chen, Wei-Lun Yen
abstract: This work introduces an electrical field based biosensor for accurate quantification of histamine, featuring a polyvinyl chloride (PVC) membrane. The device employs a highly sensitive analog front-end circuit with an 8 μV low-drift voltage offset, enabling detection over a wide concentration range of 0.02 μM to 100 μM, achieving a sensitivity of 12.35 mV/μM. When exposed to histamine in the presence of diamine oxidase (DAO), the sensor exhibited a pronounced signal change of 63%. Marked signal suppression occurs exclusively under severe interference conditions, specifically when the concentration ratio of histamine to putrescine, cadaverine, and tyramine reaches 1:20. Owing to its excellent sensitivity and selectivity, the proposed histamine biosensor is well suited for rapid, on-site histamine detection in food and fish markets, offering strong potential for food safety monitoring applications.

title: A Digital Twin Approach for Last-Mile Delivery
authors: Maria Asuncion Del Cacho Estil-Les, Wasim A. Ali, Agostino Marcello Mangini, Maria Pia Fanti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This study presents a Digital Twin (DT)-based system to solve the last-mile delivery problem. The city is divided in zones, each zone is associated to a hub where the trucks arrive and the last-mile deliveries are performed by a set of robots. The DT architecture is composed of an application layer that optimize the robot initial routes, a simulation layer connected with the physical layer to manage unexpected events, such as traffic congestion and road closures. A Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) optimization model is designed to determine the optimal routes of the robots and a simulation model is implemented for the rerouting application. The system is validated through two case studies conducted in the city of Bari, Italy, using the MILP model to reduce travel distances and energy consumption of robots and the Simulation of Urban Mobility tool for reproducing the DT physical layer. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and adaptability of the proposed approach.

title: Nonlinear Shape Control of Flexible Continuum Robots Using Offline-Online Learning with Neurodynamic Optimization
authors: Ziqi Zhuang, Zhen Deng, Chuanchuan Pan, Ying Hu, Jianwei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Precise shape control of tendon-driven continuum robots (TDCRs) remains challenging due to their inherent nonlinearity and environmental uncertainties. Analytical kinematic models fail to accurately characterize nonlinear deformation behavior. This paper proposes a model-less optimal shape control (MLOSC) framework using an offline-online learning strategy to achieve precise shape tracking under unknown loading conditions. The method approximates the TDCR’s shape Jacobian by a Radial Basis Function Neural Network (RBFNN), pretrained offline. Online adaptation of network parameters is performed via neurodynamic optimization to enhance prediction accuracy in the presence of uncertainties. The shape control problem is subsequently formulated as a constrained quadratic program (QP) that incorporates the robot’s physical limits. A neurodynamic solver is designed to compute optimal control inputs in real time. Control stability is analyzed via Lyapunov’s method. Experimental evaluations under static and dynamic loading conditions achieve average shape tracking errors of 2.04 mm (1.4% of robot total length) and 5.09 mm (3.4%), respectively. Comparative results confirm improved convergence speed and tracking accuracy over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Real-time Monocular 2D and 3D Perception of Endoluminal Scenes for Controlling Flexible Robotic Endoscopic Instruments
authors: Ruofeng Wei, Kai Chen, Yui-Lun Ng, Yiyao Ma, Justin Di-Lang Ho, Hon-Sing Tong, Xiaomei Wang, Jing Dai, Ka-Wai Kwok, Qi Dou
abstract: Endoluminal surgery offers a minimally invasive option for early-stage gastrointestinal and urinary tract, but is limited by basic surgical tools and a steep learning curve. Robotic systems, particularly continuum robots, provide flexible instruments that enable precise, intuitive tissue resection in confined spaces, potentially improving outcomes. This paper presents an integrated visual perception platform for a continuum robotic system in endoluminal surgery. Our objective is to leverage monocular endoscopic image-based perception algorithms to accurately identify the position and orientation of flexible instruments and measure their distances from surrounding tissues. This thorough understanding of continuum robots and surgical scenes enhances the robustness of robotic procedures. We introduce 2D and 3D learning-based perception algorithms and develop a physically-realistic simulator that models the dynamics of flexible instruments. This simulator features a pipeline for generating realistic endoluminal scenes, enabling control of flexible robots in a realistic environment and substantial data collection. Using a continuum robot prototype, we conducted extensive evaluations, including module assessments and system-level evaluation of the perception platform. Results demonstrate that our perception algorithms significantly improve control of flexible instruments, reducing manipulation time by over 70% for trajectory-following tasks and enhancing the understanding of complex surgical scenarios, leading to robust endoluminal surgeries.

title: Self-adaptive Retroaction-aware Representation Learning for I nductive- T ransductive Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Yufeng Zhang, Wei Chen, Victor S. Sheng, Xiaofang Zhang, An Liu, Lei Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Inductive knowledge graph completion (KGC) aims to represent unseen entities and complete triplets in emerging knowledge graphs (KGs), while the existing studies ignore that unseen elements combined with seen ones constitute a holistic new relational graph, where emerging KGs have inescapable impacts backtracking to original ones. Therefore, it is not only necessary to predict triplets in emerging KGs, but also with particular significance to further improve the completeness of original ones, considering the semantic and topological variations in the holistic new graph. To fill in this gap, we formulate a new IT (Inductive-Transductive) KGC task to transductively complete triplets inside original KGs after entities in the emerging scenario are represented and fine-tuned in an inductive manner. In order to handle this task, a novel model entitled StaR (Self-adaptive Retroaction-aware Representation) is proposed consisting of the following two modules: 1) a self-adaptive semantic encoding network is designed to adaptively adjust embeddings of seen entities to their surrounding semantic mutations; 2) a relation-aware transformer layer is developed to represent both seen and unseen entities in a unified representation space and generalize evolving reasoning paradigms to the whole graph. Our experimental results demonstrate that, compared with state-of-the-art methods, StaR is not only competitive in inductive KGC for unseen entities, but also ulteriorly improves the completeness of original parts inside the holistic new relational graph in our IT KGC task.

title: A Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM Based Cyberattack Detection Framework for IoMT and 6G Healthcare Environments
authors: Priyanka Verma, Nitesh Bharot, Prakash Kumar, Ankit Vidyarthi, John G. Breslin
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) has significantly enhanced healthcare systems but has also exposed them to sophisticated cyber threats. With the advent of 6G healthcare ecosystems, these challenges become even more critical due to ultra-dense connectivity and massive data exchange. Existing security mechanisms often fall short due to the heterogeneous nature of IoMT networks. In this work, we propose a hybrid model-based Intrusion Detection System (IDS) to secure IoMT environments. The proposed model is built using a 1D-CNN-BiLSTM architecture to accurately detect anomalies by capturing spatial patterns and temporal dependencies in network traffic. Experimental evaluations are performed on the ECU-IoHT dataset. Results demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms existing methods and achieves an accuracy of 99.40%, an F1-score of 98.95%, and a ROC-AUC score of 99.87%. These results affirm the superiority of the proposed IDS and highlight its potential as a robust security solution for safeguarding 6G healthcare-driven IoMT infrastructures.

title: Channel Knowledge Map Construction: Recent Advances and Open Challenges
authors: Zixiang Ren, Juncong Zhou, Jie Xu, Ling Qiu, Yong Zeng, Han Hu, Juyong Zhang, Rui Zhang
abstract: Channel knowledge map (CKM) has emerged as a pivotal technology for environment-aware wireless communications and sensing, which provides a priori location-specific channel knowledge to facilitate network optimization. Efficient CKM construction is an important technical problem for its effective implementation. This article provides a comprehensive overview of recent advances in CKM construction. First, we examine classical interpolation-based CKM construction methods, highlighting their limitations in practical deployments. Next, we explore image processing and generative artificial intelligence (AI) techniques, which leverage feature extraction to construct CKMs based on environmental knowledge. Furthermore, we present emerging wireless radiance field (WRF) frameworks that exploit neural radiance fields or Gaussian splatting to construct high-fidelity CKMs from sparse measurement data. Finally, we outline various future research directions in real-time and cross-domain CKM construction, as well as cost-efficient deployment of CKMs.

title: Active Domain Adaptation for Mmwave-Based HAR Via Rényi Entropy-Based Uncertainty Estimation
authors: Mingzhi Lin, Teng Huang, Han Ding, Cui Zhao, Fei Wang, Ge Wang, Wei Xi
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using mmWave radar provides a non-invasive alternative to traditional sensor-based methods but suffers from domain shift, where model performance declines in new users, positions, or environments. To address this, we propose mmADA, an Active Domain Adaptation (ADA) framework that efficiently adapts mmWave-based HAR models with minimal labeled data. mmADA enhances adaptation by introducing Rényi Entropy-based uncertainty estimation to identify and label the most informative target samples. Additionally, it leverages contrastive learning and pseudo-labeling to refine feature alignment using unlabeled data. Evaluations with a TI IWR1443BOOST radar across multiple users, positions, and environments show that mmADA achieves over 90% accuracy in various cross-domain settings. Comparisons with five baselines confirm its superior adaptation performance, while further tests on unseen users, environments, and two additional open-source datasets validate its robustness and generalization.

abstract: It is our great honor to present the proceedings of the 2025 5th International Conference on Robotics, Automation, and Artificial Intelligence (RAAI 2025), which was held at Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, within December 18-20, 2025. This conference has brought together leading experts, researchers, and practitioners from over 30 countries, including China, the United States, Singapore, Germany, South Korea, Japan, Italy, Canada, the United Kingdom, India, Thailand, South Africa and many others. The esteemed academic environment of NTU has provided an ideal setting for fostering innovation, intellectual exchange, and global collaboration.
